However, this is a pure diagnostic/probe without a repair arm, no dimension reaches 8+, and the difference is not established at clear-accept level, so novelty is capped at borderline; SA is unavailable.

column4: Remove the load-bearing assumption that effective manipulation demonstrations must be generated by real-time closed-loop teleoperation on the target robot. If an equal amount of human-only offline demonstration, retargeted and calibrated with very little robot data, matches pure real-time teleoperation BC, real-time in-loop demonstration is not load-bearing.
column6: CRITICAL F1/direct-hit: HumanEgo (2605.24934) directly demonstrates the headline under matched collection time: human video outperforms teleoperated ACT by 41%; it also argues that the budget should not be spent on teleoperation rigs. The prior-work review finds high overlap, and although three crack items match, break-glass① fails because headline occupancy is nonzero. The controlled difference of ≤10% calibration and the same BC head is insufficient for clear-accept.

column4: Remove the load-bearing assumption that a VLA visual tower must be initialized with internet image-text contrastive weights from the CLIP/SigLIP family. Under matched architecture and robot-data budget, a robot-video SSL or from-scratch visual tower that matches SigLIP initialization would show that web contrastive initialization is not load-bearing.
column6: CRITICAL/F1: R3M/MVP/ACT already weaken the claim that CLIP/SigLIP image-text initialization is required. With high overlap, a matched-budget three-arm VLA study is a nearby transfer ablation rather than a clear-accept-level difference.

column4: Remove the assumption that standard manipulation-policy evaluation must use independently reset i.i.d. episodes as its atomic unit. Continuous long-session evaluation without reset should reverse rankings relative to i.i.d. evaluation, showing that resets hide false generalization dependent on human cleanup.
column6: Both exception conditions for the load-bearing-assumption form fail: medium overlap and only 1/2 cracks match. The headline experiment is separable from EARL/AutoEval, but novelty is capped and cannot receive SA. The falsification is executable and has no CRITICAL finding.

column4: Planning degradation in world models is attributed to pixel/latent drift, but an alternative is that the planner’s effective exploration radius in action dimensions shrinks exponentially with horizon. At equal reconstruction error, increasing action-side temperature should improve planning more than further reducing reconstruction error.
column6: MAJOR: the estimand is misaligned—increasing action temperature≠showing that conditional action entropy shrinks exponentially with horizon. MAJOR: the difference from the “reconstruction≠plannability” occupancy in World-in-World/RC-aux is insufficient for clear-accept.

column4: Human pairwise preferences label the recoverability margin near the annotation moment rather than global trajectory quality. A local-window preference model should predict perturbed failure better than trajectory-level preference and improve weighted BC only in high-risk phases.
column6: MAJOR: the headline “human labels represent recoverability margin” is misaligned with the signal “local labels better predict irreversible failure under perturbation.” Preference Transformer and related work occupy the neighborhood, so novelty is capped; SA is unavailable.

column4: Remove the load-bearing assumption that ablation and model selection must rely on a privileged environment success detector as the sole ground truth. If rankings under onboard-observable success estimates reverse relative to an oracle, oracle-ground-truth evaluation is not load-bearing and paper conclusions do not transfer to deployable observations.
column6: The load-bearing SA route fails because the overlap is medium, and only 1 crack matches. AutoEval/SIMPLER already occupy ranking consistency, while a reversal can be confounded with a poor onboard detector (estimand MAJOR).

column4: Current VLA “held-out / zero-shot” success rates mix novel visual instances with novel task-structure compositions into one generalization score. The metric measures a mixture rather than structural generalization; separating the axes should reverse method rankings and change model selection.
column6: The prior-work review finds high overlap: Inductive Gen/STAR-Gen/LIBERO-Plus already separate appearance vs structure. Ranking reversal only strengthens the prediction and provides no clear-accept-level difference (F1 CRITICAL).

column4: Open-loop reconstruction MSE/FDE in action-representation papers measures trajectory-geometry fidelity, not error amplification under closed-loop perturbation. At equal MSE, amplification can differ by an order of magnitude and reverse representation selection.
column6: ActionCodec already occupies “reconstruction≠good representation.” The comparison of equal MSE and A is viable, but medium novelty is insufficient for SA. Matching MSE across representations is itself difficult to control, adding an experiment/baseline MAJOR.

column4: Define “success-boundary thickness”: the distribution of shortest task-metric distance from a successful terminal state to failure. Policies with thin boundaries score artificially high under evaluation noise; boundary-thickening augmentation should improve worst-quantile success only on thin-boundary tasks.
column6: The prior-work review finds medium overlap, with energy margin/CV boundary thickness occupying “margin to failure.” Operationalizing δ and the ranking-reversal signal remain confounded, leaving the idea below clear-accept.

column4: Define a “memory-necessity map”: counterfactually perturb historical observations by lag and measure the decay profile of action KL with τ. Memory actually consumed is much sparser than the context window, so keyframe memory should repair long-horizon failure better than an equally long full history.
column6: The repair arm is occupied isomorphically by KEMO/BPP and related work. The scramble_τ KL is misaligned with the “memory necessity” estimand because it is confounded by OOD inputs.

column4: Define “scene-identity shortcut extractability”: the fraction of action variance explained by a linear probe using only background/scene ID. A high shortcut rate should predict sim2real degradation better than pixel-domain distance, and shortcut removal should reduce the gap.
column6: CRITICAL F1: high overlap. 2508.06426 already covers definition, measurement, and repair of scene/background shortcuts; R only rewrites the metric.

column4: Remove the load-bearing assumption that visual tokens and action tokens must share one Transformer sequence with joint self-attention. Vision emits only a slowly varying scene code, while an independent short-sequence action tower decodes actions; matching the joint sequence at equal data would show joint self-attention is not load-bearing.
column6: CRITICAL F1: the prior-work review finds high overlap, and the GR00T family and dual-system architectures already instantiate an independent action tower. The residual “match at equal budget” is insufficient for clear-accept, and the load-bearing SA route closes because overlap is not low.

column4: Multitask VLA co-training couples shared manipulation geometry and task-language binding in one backward pass. Splitting it into a language-free geometry-primitive tower and language-binding head with frozen geometry should localize most task interference to the binding head.
column6: A two-stage residual remains viable at medium overlap. The MAJOR finding is a measurement mismatch: conflict counts after the split≠the binding location before the split. No dimension reaches 8+, so the idea is not SA.

column4: Cross-camera extrinsic transfer and cross-embodiment kinematic transfer are studied separately, but both are “perception–execution coordinate-map reregistration.” One chart-mismatch probe should predict degradation on both axes and reverse source selection performed separately for each.
column6: The medium-overlap residual is chart-mismatch and ranking reversal, which is viable. MAJOR: as a pure probe/source-selection diagnostic, its ceiling is borderline, and tension between the axes in Data Analogies weakens the unified estimand.

column4: Remove the load-bearing convention that the training-time success filter must be isomorphic to the evaluation success predicate. After separation, training can retain recoverable failure segments while evaluation still uses strict terminal success, improving both data efficiency and deployment alignment.
column6: Axiom-removal condition ① fails because the prior-work review finds medium overlap rather than low. The difference from SSDF/failure-trajectory clusters is below clear-accept. The requirement of ≥2 matching cracks is met and the falsification can kill the wager, so cap at AwR.

column4: Deciding when a robot asks for help and when it autonomously aborts are implemented separately, but both are threshold specializations of one remaining-task risk budget under two parameters: the price of human attention and the price of task failure. A unified estimator should move smoothly along the help-rate–abort-rate frontier as prices change.
column6: Thrifty already gates help using one risk estimate. An abort and λ sweep over a Pareto frontier does not provide a clear-accept-level difference (medium overlap). The falsification is executable; cap at AwR.

column4: Demonstration cleaning and curriculum ordering are treated as separate pipelines, but both assign training weights to demonstration segments. Under a unified learnability-density weight, the product baseline of independent top-k cleaning×independent curriculum should be strictly dominated at equal tokens.
column6: MAJOR×2: ① SR dominance≠proof that “two stages are a strictly suboptimal decomposition of w,” an estimand mismatch; ② medium overlap vs DataMIL and IMC leaves no clear-accept difference. At the recorded “≥2MAJOR” threshold, cap at AwR.

column4: Evolution: failures on quantities and quantifiers are primarily caused by the action head lacking a remaining-count progress representation. Add a head-to-head test against the opposite attribution in 2602.24143, and expand evaluation to N>4 and multiobject counting across suites.
column6: The quantifier-failure diagnosis and remaining-count token repair and a four-arm head-to-head against 2602.24143 are publishable. The prior-work audit reviewed 6 papers and assigns medium overlap. Neighbors on progress/remaining-steps (2603.09292/2511.14759) keep the headline mechanism below clear-accept; no dimension reliably reaches 8, so the verdict is AwR and SA is unavailable.

column4: Remove the load-bearing assumption that a visual encoder must be co-trained with the action head under the same BC/joint loss. If a frozen strong visual SSL representation with only the action head trained matches end-to-end co-training, joint task gradients are not load-bearing.
column6: F1 CRITICAL: the frozen-vision vs co-trained closed-loop comparison is directly occupied by 2409.20248 (large degradation with frozen vision) and KI 2505.23705 (≈0% with a frozen VLA backbone). The prior-work review finds high overlap. Narrowing to a DINOv2/SigLIP-VLA stack is not a clear-accept difference. Crack verification found only 1 match +1 partial, extinguishing both break-glass ①②, so Reject.

column4: “When to pause demonstration” during teleoperation collection and “when to request takeover” during deployment are studied separately, but both estimate interruption value under scarce human attention. Collection pause points should predict deployment help points zero-shot.
column6: The unified estimand of collection pause as deployment help has problem-definition value and no one-paper occupant (medium overlap, the API search for teleop+pause+intervention returned 0 results); falsification is executable. However, pause weak labels≠V_int is a confound (MAJOR), while the help-request literature is dense and the ceiling is low (MAJOR), so ≥2 MAJOR findings cap the result at AwR.

column4: Evolution: allocate reasoning depth by proximity to irreversibility. Add head-to-head comparisons with GeCO/generic difficulty gating and a permutation attribution arm to show that gains occur only near irreversible states rather than on generally difficult steps.
column6: Adaptive compute by difficulty is occupied by DASIP/DeeR/AC²/RD-VLA (medium overlap). Replacing the gating axis with irreversibility and the permutation arm is not a clear-accept-level difference, and the baseline must be DASIP rather than the misnamed GeCO, so F1 and F3 produce two MAJOR findings and caps the result at AwR. The minimal falsification is executable, so the idea does not warrant rejection.

column4: Remove the assumption that autonomous real-robot data collection and evaluation rollouts require a human safety operator continuously holding a deadman control. Under unattended firmware-level workspace walls and torque/contact cutoffs, if emergency-stop rate does not exceed the staffed baseline and valid-trajectory throughput rises, continuous human watch is not load-bearing.
column6: The headline “continuous human watch is removable by moving safety into firmware barriers” is substantially occupied by unattended nighttime QT-Opt and AutoDex residual-torque autonomous loops and throughput (the prior-work review finds high overlap). All four load-bearing-removal conditions fail because occupancy is not low and only 1 crack matches +1 is partial, so F1 CRITICAL novelty-dead; no upgrade.

column4: Contact-phase failure of discrete action tokens is caused not by quantization-error magnitude, but by token boundaries cutting continuous force ramps. A continuous residual during contact should improve only force-ramp tasks.
column6: Discrete failure and contact-phase residual streams are separately occupied by FAST, PhaForce, and M2 (medium overlap). The force-ramp-specific causal attribution is below clear-accept, while success-conditional improvement≠the estimand that an impulse was cut by a token boundary, so F1 and the estimand give two MAJOR findings and caps the result at AwR. The falsification is executable, so the idea does not warrant rejection.

column4: Recheck: cross-embodiment transfer gains are confounded by the target embodiment’s intrinsic difficulty. Normalize for difficulty, test whether source-embodiment rankings reverse, and use the corrected ranking for source selection; resubmit unchanged after reading cross-embodiment evaluation papers in full.
column6: The prior-work review finds medium overlap: Data Analogies already uses a target upper bound/absolute success rate, while no single paper covers the full chain “difficulty-normalized gain drives ranking reversal and source selection.” The difference is unlikely to reach clear-accept. The search is sufficient with 8 papers read. A source-selection repair is attached and falsification is executable, so the verdict is AwR, with novelty capped and SA unavailable.

column4: Remove the assumption that VLA/BC training must optimize strictly causal stepwise action likelihood. Distill a strictly causal student from a noncausal teacher that sees k future frames. If the student substantially outperforms purely causal BC under causal deployment constraints, strict causality during training is not load-bearing.
column6: The axiom-removal break-glass route lacks ①: the prior-work review finds medium overlap, and X-Morph already distills noncausal or privileged information into a causal student. The crack audit records 2× matches, and the falsification can kill the wager. In ordinary form, the F1 mechanism family is occupied and transfer from a future-frame VLA teacher is not shown sufficiently nontrivial, so the verdict is AwR and SA is unavailable.

column4: Current compositional-generalization leaderboards mix “unseen predicate–object Cartesian combinations” with cases where coverage of the training atoms is itself empty. After naming the atomic-coverage gap, adding atomic demonstrations should repair composition failure better than adding random composed demonstrations.
column6: CRITICAL F1: the diagnostic headline is directly occupied by ATOM-Bench AS/CFS (high overlap), while the atomic-data repair is nearly a corollary of the diagnosis and adjacent to Gao composition-aware collection.

column4: Recheck: measure a demonstration-replay ceiling for every embodied benchmark—the success-rate upper bound from replay under the benchmark’s own predicate—to correct apparent SOTA saturation. After controlling for task difficulty, test whether replay failure predicts per-task BC degradation.
column6: OpenVLA regenerate already replays under the benchmark predicate and records success; the prior-work evidence indicates medium overlap. Promoting c to benchmark metadata and the difficulty-controlled BC prediction is different but not established at clear-accept, so novelty is capped. As a pure diagnostic/change of evaluation target without a repair arm, no dimension reaches 8+; the verdict is borderline. Because the falsification is executable and not dominated, the verdict is AwR and SA is unavailable.

column4: Remove the assumption that a latent state in a manipulation world model must support high-fidelity observation reconstruction to be valid for planning. If a state preserving only control-separable or value-equivalent dimensions matches a reconstruction-based WM in planning success, observation sufficiency is not load-bearing.
column6: CRITICAL F1: the manipulation-domain headline “remove observation reconstruction as load-bearing and judge by planning success” is directly occupied by Semantic World Models (2510.19818) and V-JEPA 2-AC (2506.09985); the prior-work review finds high overlap. Only VE/bisimulation vs VQA/JEPA and equal-parameter and PSNR protocol details remain, which are insufficient for clear-accept. Axiom-removal SA condition ①, low overlap, fails and does not exempt the high-overlap hard gate.

column4: The global relative vs absolute action-representation choice is wrong: representation quality depends on the coordinate frame containing the task constraint. Absolute actions should win for world-frame constraints and relative actions for hand/tool-frame constraints; selecting by constraint frame should reverse the global winner.
column6: Demystifying (2602.23408) already gives large-scale global abs/delta conclusions, and SRT shows that changing the reference frame changes the winner (prior-work evidence indicating medium overlap). The proposition “constraint frame as switch with crossed-interaction reversal” is real and measurable, but the residual is not shown at clear-accept level, so novelty is capped. The falsification, task pairs×three heads×interaction and gating, is executable on one GPU, so the verdict is AwR and SA is unavailable.

column4: Remove the assumption that open-loop action-chunk length H must be fixed globally before training. If state-conditioned variable H—short during contact and long in free space—improves success at both ends under equal FLOPs, globally fixed H is not load-bearing.
column6: The prior-work review finds high overlap: AAC/ACH/MDPI already remove “globally fixed H.” Only 1/2 cracks match, so the framing is compliant; the residual controller/equal-FLOPs protocol is insufficient for clear-accept.

column4: The main benefit from a larger action-token vocabulary is not finer quantization, but increased coverage of rare contact tokens. Entropy-matched resampling of rare actions should let a fixed small vocabulary match a large one, while uniform vocabulary expansion without resampling should not.
column6: The coverage vs quantization causal account and entropy-matched resampling falsification is unoccupied, but the medium neighborhood around ActionCodec/long-tail BC caps SA. An attached repair arm reaches borderline.

column4: Chunk failures are attributed to stale observations, but an alternative is visual-sampling phase misalignment with contact events. At the same visual bandwidth, concentrating the frame budget near predicted contact should improve contact success, while uniformly raising FPS without phase alignment should not.
column6: The same-bandwidth φ-versus-τ phase-alignment comparison has no single-paper occupant, but the RDP/PhaForce/event neighborhood gives medium overlap and caps SA. The repair arm is clear enough for borderline.

column4: Recheck: human-in-the-loop deployment evaluation treats intervention rate and autonomous duration as objective metrics, but the person pressing the intervention button is an uncalibrated instrument. Measure how much inter-supervisor variance contributes to cross-method differences and provide a reproducible standardized synthetic-supervisor protocol.
column6: This is pure diagnostic and instrument calibration with medium overlap; 2601.15069/HIL-SERL/AGPS already describe supervisor variance. It has no repair arm and no dimension reaches 8+, so cap at borderline.

column4: Remove the assumption that deployment episodes fed back into BC training must first pass success/no-collision filtering. At equal tokens, retaining task-relevant contacts and pre-failure prefixes should dominate the success-filtered set on long-tail SR, showing that success filtering is not load-bearing.
column6: CRITICAL F1: high overlap, and the SSDF/failure-demo family already covers “failures enter BC.” The cracks are 1 partial +1 mismatch, so the four axiom-removal conditions are incomplete. The equal-token three-arm design is only an experimental refinement.

column4: Contact-phase BC fails not because action-supervision density is low, but because supervision uses the wrong clock: stepwise action time instead of contact-event time. With targets aligned to contact-event timestamps and phase-conditioned actions, equal-token training should beat stepwise BC; adding the same number of demonstrations without changing alignment should not.
column6: The proposition is falsifiable and includes a repair arm, but the overlap is medium because the neighborhood around phase-conditioned ACT/AWE/contact-window oversampling is dense. The difference is below clear-accept level, so novelty is capped.

column4: Remove the assumption that policy comparison must use mean success from independently resampled episodes as the sole protocol. Paired comparisons from the same initial state or common random numbers should have strictly greater decision power at equal machine time.
column6: Paired/CRN/sequential strict comparison is occupied by Strens and N-SCORE. Greater power at equal budget≠the headline that the protocol is legitimate, and the four axiom-removal SA conditions are incomplete because the overlap is medium.

column4: Define “within-episode failure-conditioned shift”: after one failed attempt, the policy’s conditioning distribution becomes systematically worse and begins to thrash. Shift magnitude should predict terminal failure, and a failure-condition recovery head should repair it better than adding the same amount of successful demonstration.
column6: Within-episode failure detection/recovery is occupied by To Err/FAR. A Δ_fail correlation≠the causal headline that final failure is caused by amplified shift.

column4: Define “referential competition entropy”: entropy over the number of objects matching an instruction. Failure rises with H rather than single-object grounding accuracy; explicit marginalization over referents should repair it better than more language data.
column6: An H_ref-conditioned slope and marginalization arm is feasible, but interactive-disambiguation work is occupied by INVIGORATE/information-theoretic querying. The difference is below clear-accept.

column4: Remove the assumption that a policy and VLM-judge must share a visual backbone for alignment and compute efficiency. Force the reward tower to see only raw observations, not policy intermediate activations; if post-training gains and resistance to hack are no worse than with sharing, sharing is not load-bearing.
column6: The axiom-removal route lacks conditions ①②: medium overlap rather than low; both verified cracks are “partial,” with 0 “matches”; the review therefore evaluates it strictly as an ordinary form. RoboReward/SOLE-R1 already occupy an independent VLM reward, raw-observation-only input, and weights separate from the policy. The headline “shared visual backbone is load-bearing” looks more like actor-critic sharing than a VLM-judge default in prior-work evidence, creating strawman risk. F1 and failed cracks give 2 MAJOR findings, below a clear-accept ceiling; no upgrade.

column4: Safety-filter degradation is usually attributed to an overly risky policy; an alternative is that CBF/QP projection geometry becomes ill-conditioned where multiple constraints intersect, independently of the task policy. With the policy frozen, repairing projection should improve the safety–task tradeoff more than changing only the policy under frozen projection.
column6: Hierarchical CBF/relaxation already covers projection-side repair. The residual is a crossed frozen-component causal separation, with medium overlap and an insufficient clear-accept difference.

column4: Open-loop action chunks couple action content and commitment in one generation. Split them into a content stream and a commit stream. Proposition: most open-loop failures come from misplaced commit boundaries rather than wrong content; repairing only commit should reduce failure more than longer chunks or full replanning.
column6: SV-VLA/PACE/DVAC densely occupy commit/replan mechanisms. The residual is a causal experiment that freezes content and trains only commit; novelty is capped.

column4: Remove the load-bearing assumption that near-failure or contact recovery must regenerate action content by flushing the chunk and rerunning the VLA. If time-reparameterizing only the current chunk—local slowdown or inserted pauses—rescues most of the failures recovered by content replanning, content regeneration is not load-bearing.
column6: The axiom-removal route closes: prior-work evidence verification finds 2/2 cracks mismatched (2210.07632 is queueing-network work; 2402.00807 is offline RL; neither concerns slowdown/asynchrony), so the framing is compliant but evaluated as an ordinary form. The prior-work overlap is medium because BSP already provides execution-level temporal rescaling with zero large-model forward passes. The difference, “slow down failure vs speed up success,” cannot alone support clear accept. 2 MAJOR findings cap the result at AwR; the A/B/C falsification can kill the wager, so the idea does not warrant rejection.

column4: The success gain from action chunks is caused not by “temporal consistency or suppression of myopia,” but by low-pass filtering high-frequency teleoperation jitter. A causal low-pass single-step BC arm with matched group delay should match chunking, while an unfiltered single-step arm with the same delay should not.
column6: B≈A shows only that matched-delay low-pass filtering is sufficient; sufficiency≠causation, so the estimand is misaligned. This adds a MAJOR. The prior-work overlap is medium because ACT TE/BID/FAFM already connect chunking to smoothing/frequency. The matched-group-delay head-to-head is a better ablation but unlikely to reach clear accept. 2 MAJOR findings plus the diagnostic ceiling cap the result at AwR; A/B/C is executable and decisive, so the idea does not warrant rejection.

column4: Closed-loop degradation under visual latency is caused not by image content becoming too stale for planning, but by breaking the visual–proprioceptive phase alignment seen in training. Under the same wall-clock visual delay, preserving training-time proprioceptive–action phase should hurt substantially less than the same delay with phase disorder.
column6: The B, visual-delay-only, vs C, both modalities with the same Δ, comparison is clean and differs from the MMDR headline. However, the prior-work review finds medium overlap because MMDR/DAM-VLA/DuoCore occupy asynchronous multimodal latency. The repair arm, a phase buffer/timestamp token, is established engineering, no dimension reliably reaches 8, and clear-accept remains doubtful. The verdict is AwR with 1 MAJOR finding; the idea is not rejected because the falsifiable experiment, repair arm, and complete search have no direct hit.

column4: Held-out success rates for language-conditioned VLAs treat instruction-template wording as noise, but the template family is an unreported degree of freedom. Variance under paraphrase alone can consume cross-method gaps; the current metric measures default-template engineering rather than skill.
column6: Prescreening found direct occupancy: https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.28301.

column4: Remove the assumption that manipulation/VLA papers must use task macro-average success as the sole primary aggregate. If worst-k or deployment-weighted aggregation reverses SOTA and the original winner is systematically worst in the tail, the single macro scalar is not load-bearing.
column6: The load-bearing-assumption SA exception stops at medium overlap because ① fails. RLiable already occupies “one aggregate scalar is insufficient,” while ranking reversal under a task-bag worst-k is only borderline evaluation science.

column4: The “imagined-trajectory diversity” metric for an interactive WM measures injected-noise width rather than reachable-set coverage and is decoupled from IoU with the true reachable set. Only reachable-set-aligned coverage should improve planning; raising noise diversity should not.
column6: The prior-work review finds low overlap, and the idea has a repair arm, but the main contribution is a diagnostic probe and the reachable-set IoU definition is brittle. It is unlikely to reach clear-accept, so SA is unavailable.

column4: Reported “gain over a BC baseline” treats undertraining of the baseline as method contribution. After training the baseline for equal optimization steps to its validation plateau, the reported relative gain should systematically collapse; the current metric measures room left by a weak baseline.
column6: The prior-work review finds medium overlap because Implementation Matters/RL that Matters already show attribution errors in reported gains. An IL relative-gain plateau protocol is a quantitative formalization of reproducibility common sense, with a borderline ceiling.

column4: Closed-loop VLA systems have “language-side silent hijacking”: adversarial paraphrases or inserted clauses can systematically rewrite action intent while vision is unchanged. They require task-agnostic language–action consistency monitoring and refusal.
column6: Prescreening found direct occupancy: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.12978.

column4: A precision-manipulation VLA is not load-bearing on a wrist camera. Third-person monocular vision and viewpoint augmentation should match a wrist and third-person dual-view baseline on contact tasks, allowing the wrist-camera stream to be removed entirely.
column6: The load-bearing form is falsifiable and passes the forcing-constraint and falsification checks, but the prior-work review finds medium overlap: Look Closer/WristWorld already quantify wrist-camera gains. Zero headline occupancy fails and the difference is unlikely to reach clear-accept; cap at AwR.

column4: VLA instability under synonymous instructions is caused not by grounding capacity, but by language-manifold collapse from low training-template entropy. Increasing only instruction-template entropy, without changing vision or actions, should selectively improve paraphrase generalization.
column6: Paraphrase instability and language augmentation is largely occupied by LangGap/LIBERO-Para. The isolation test for “template entropy rather than capacity” leaves a residual, but no dimension reaches 8+. The estimand “manifold collapse” is also misaligned with paraphrase accuracy; ≥2 MAJOR findings cap the result at AwR.

column4: The ACT-style temporal ensemble is not load-bearing for deployment. Remove weighted averaging of overlapping chunks, execute only the first τ steps under a receding-horizon policy, and increase replanning frequency; static tasks should match the baseline and dynamic tasks should improve.
column6: The prior-work review finds high overlap: removing TE and using a receding prefix is already standard in DP and occupied by RTC/TAS. The residual—a four-arm falsification under the same checkpoint plus outperformance on dynamic tasks—is insufficient for clear-accept and is novelty-dead.

column4: Remove the assumption that simulation manipulation benchmarks must lock mass, friction, and controller gains to one published default point and report rankings only there. The default parameter point is an undeclared load-bearing component; if neighborhood perturbations reverse SOTA, the single-point leaderboard is not load-bearing.
column6: The load-bearing SA route fails because the overlap is medium≠low and only 1 crack matches +1 is partial. SIMPLER already reports a mass×friction grid with MMRV≈0, capping F1 novelty. The expected signal is weakened by the nearest-neighbor prior.

column4: Current sim2real gap metrics use the absolute success-rate drop, ΔSR, as “domain distance,” conflating intrinsic task difficulty with domain shift. A difficulty-normalized transfer coefficient is the actual domain gap; ΔSR rankings and normalized rankings should systematically reverse and change model selection.
column6: There is medium overlap with SIMPLER’s absolute SR and grasp transfer ratio. τ=SR_real/SR_sim is misaligned with the estimand “difficulty-normalized domain distance” (MAJOR), leaving an unstable clear-accept ceiling.

column4: Averaging action-conditioned world-model prediction error over expert actions measures “imitation information along demonstrations,” not the OOD action branches queried by a planner. Models with low expert-trajectory error should reverse error rankings on planning actions; selecting by planning-action error is required to improve planning success.
column6: CRITICAL F1: the prior-work review finds high overlap and aligns with the Operator-on-F/VE headline. The difference is only a narrower manipulation slice and lacks clear-accept-level distinctiveness.

column4: Remove the assumption that manipulation VLA/BC actions must map to rigid position or velocity servo setpoints. If the policy directly outputs Cartesian or joint impedance—stiffness, damping, and target—it should dominate a same-data position-servo baseline in contact-task SR and peak force without degrading free-space behavior.
column6: CRITICAL F1: high overlap. The headline “replace rigid position setpoints with variable impedance” is substantially occupied by ACP/CompliantVLA/VICES, and the remaining delta is below clear-accept.

column4: Remove the assumption that sim2real visual policies require photorealistic rendering or high-fidelity appearance. Nonphotorealistic geometry rendering and dynamics/sensor-noise randomization should match a path-traced visual DR pipeline under zero-shot real-robot transfer.
column6: CRITICAL F1: high overlap. Tobin/CAD2RL/SimNet long ago broke the claim that photorealism is necessary. Only 1 crack matches +1 is partial, so the axiom-removal route fails.

column4: Remove the assumption that high-quality teleoperation demonstrations require real-time robot-camera video feedback to the operator. Equal amounts of demonstrations collected using only proprioceptive/force feedback, without a robot-camera stream, should train BC that matches video teleoperation on contact tasks.
column6: The A/B demonstration-to-BC comparison has no direct-hit, but the overlap is medium; both cracks are “partial,” and the increment over DOGlove is small. Two MAJOR findings cap the result at AwR, below SA.

column4: Remove the assumption that a multitask or large-data VLA must jointly update its shared visual encoder end to end. If a frozen shared backbone and lightweight task-conditioned adapters dominates full end-to-end training on negative-transfer task families without hurting positive-transfer tasks, end-to-end shared updates are not load-bearing.
column6: Prescreening found direct occupancy: https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.09298.

column4: Recheck: unlabeled reset/cleanup segments in teleoperation corpora are learned as task actions; reset-segment share predicts terminal reversal rate, and filtering those segments repairs the failure.
column6: The prior-work review finds high overlap. HELP/SCIZOR already cover the main path of “filter non-task/idle segments, then retrain.” The difference—reset sharing with a terminal-reversal and filtering arm—does not reach an independent clear-accept-level contribution (F1 CRITICAL).

column4: World-model planning degradation is caused not by stepwise dynamics error, but by the planner assuming it can change actions every step while deployment commits to open-loop action chunks. Forcing planning to obey the commit window should improve planning success at the same MSE.
column6: The same-MSE commit-H vs stepwise-MPC test is executable and not a pure probe, but nearby commit or macro-action planning work such as HWM makes the method arm thin; novelty is insufficient for SA.

column4: Multistep consistency training improves open-loop video fidelity on expert-action branches, not counterfactual-action plannability queried by the planner. Multistep loss on planning or random-action branches should predict planning gains better than expert-branch loss; reducing only expert-branch multistep loss should not improve planning.
column6: The expert-branch vs counterfactual-branch multistep estimand and reversal training arm is clean, but neighbors on Objective Mismatch/multistep-objective mismatch cap novelty below the clear-accept ceiling.

column4: Evolution: recompute only the physical-constraint residual frontier in an object-centric world model. Add strong Mixture-of-Depths/learned-router baselines and a FLOPs-matched head-to-head, narrowing the sole surviving signal to additional savings from the constraint frontier relative to MoD.
column6: Physical-constraint residual gating relative to MoD has medium overlap with Adaptive-Compute-WM/MoD/SPARTAN, suppressing the separable increment below clear-accept.

column4: Remove the assumption that training and standard evaluation must use the same fixed control or action-issue rate as the dataset. A single-rate leaderboard treats frequency as a hidden load-bearing component; if cross-rate reevaluation reverses SOTA, the single-rate protocol is not load-bearing, and multirate augmentation should repair frequency-drift failures better than a larger backbone.
column6: The axiom-removal SA route fails because the overlap is medium and only 1 crack matches. Variable-rate RL/multirate chunks already show that fixed control rate can be relaxed; the evaluation-reversal increment is borderline.

column4: Current VLA selection and leaderboards use mean success for central tendency, while deployment depends on the tail across initial states. Proposition: mean ranking and CVaR/p10 ranking systematically reverse; mean-selected SOTA is worst in the bottom quantile, and a lower-tail calibration objective repairs deployment failure better than further increasing the mean.
column6: On Bernoulli outcomes across initial states, the CVaR/p10 estimand can degenerate (F6). PhAIL/RAIL/RLiable provide medium overlap, and the lower-tail repair is insufficient for clear-accept.

column4: Remove the assumption that shared human–robot control must use real-time blending in low-level action space. If arbitration only at the intent or subgoal level with fully autonomous low-level control matches action blending under the same takeover budget, action-level blending is not load-bearing.
column6: The break-glass route lacks two of four conditions: the prior-work review finds medium overlap rather than low, and both cracks are “partial” rather than ≥2 “matches.” F1 has medium overlap because 2511.08912 already implements planning-level intent arbitration while human intervention remains shared DWA and because the crack evidence is partial, producing 2 MAJOR findings and an AwR cap. The minimal falsification can kill the wager and has no CRITICAL finding, so the idea does not warrant rejection.

column4: The value of human takeover lies not in corrected action content, but in labeling the current irreversible boundary. Distilling only a boundary map, without corrected trajectories, should reduce irreversible failure more under the same number of takeovers.
column6: F1 has medium overlap: EIL/HG-DAgger/PVP already formalize intervention timing as a value or safety boundary, aligned with the headline “boundary information>corrected trajectories.” F3: the strongest baselines name only correction BC/DAgger and omit EIL/PVP, so 2 MAJOR findings cap the result at AwR. Boundary-only vs correction BC under equal takeover budget has no one-paper occupant and is executable, with no CRITICAL finding, so the idea does not warrant rejection.

column4: For sim2real, degradation is attributed to visual domain shift, but an alternative is mismatch in control-interface latency distributions. Matching the real latency distribution in simulation should close the gap better than visual DR; visual DR without latency changes should not.
column6: F1 CRITICAL: the prior-work review of sim2real finds high overlap. Peng 2018 (1710.06537) substantially occupies the headline “command-to-actuation latency mismatch is the main cause and should be matched in simulation” through action-timestep randomization and a real-robot ablation (0.89 to 0.29). The residual, a four-condition head-to-head vs visual DR, is an evaluation slice rather than a clear-accept-level new proposition; novelty-dead and directly Reject.

column4: Remove the assumption that a manipulation world model used for planning must generate future observations or video open-loop. If a nongenerative planner that maintains only action-discriminative latent dynamics and preferences matches a generative WM with MPC under the same data, future-observation generation is not load-bearing.
column6: CRITICAL F1: the prior-work review finds high overlap. The headline “manipulation WM planning need not generate future observations open-loop” is occupied in principle by MuZero/value equivalence and in continuous-control manipulation by decoder-free latent planning in TD-MPC/TD-MPC2. Only experimental packaging with the same data and FLOPs and a placebo decoder remains, which is insufficient for clear-accept. Axiom-removal break-glass condition ①, low overlap, fails and grants no exemption.

column4: Self-excited oscillation of a closed-loop VLA in a robot arm’s structural resonance band is a safety failure omitted by success rate. Oscillation spectral power independently predicts hardware risk, and a spectrum-shaping loss should reduce oscillation without necessarily increasing SR.
column6: The entire headline—structural-resonance spectral power as an SR-orthogonal safety estimand and VLA self-excitation—has no one-paper prior-work occupant (medium overlap), and the falsification includes a repair arm. However, (1) the default rigid Franka simulation has no structural mode, creating an estimand-executability MAJOR; (2) CAPS/input-shaping each covers half, and the clear-accept difference from smoothing losses is insufficient, so F1 MAJOR. At ≥2 MAJOR, cap at AwR; nonzero mechanism-transfer occupancy prevents SA.

column4: Cross-embodiment negative transfer is often attributed to arm kinematics, but an alternative is mismatch between gripper travel and object-feature size. After normalizing this scale ratio, gains from a kinematic adapter should collapse.
column6: The competing explanation “ρ=travel/object-feature-size mismatch>arm kinematics” and the collapse of adapter gains are testable. The prior-work review finds medium overlap, and Mirage does not perform partial correlation or test gain collapse. The falsification is executable by one researcher on 1×H100. F1 MAJOR: Mirage already quantifies gripper size/matched width, making formalization by ρ a thin clear-accept increment. No dimension reliably reaches 8 and occupancy is nonzero; the verdict is AwR; SA is unavailable.

column4: Proposition: a closed-loop VLA does not truly consume the observation at every step; a large fraction of its actions are generated by motion autoregression—an autopilot mode. Define an “autopilot score” from the share of action variance under a frozen-observation counterfactual, test whether it predicts perturbation-recovery failure better than perception metrics, and reduce it through observation-forcing training. If the signal is absent, the “closed loop at every step” assumption survives and the wager dies.
column6: The prior-work review finds high overlap: the headline phenomenon is occupied by SV-VLA/A2C2/SilentDrift. The difference between the score and observation forcing is below clear-accept. Only 1 crack matches, and the baselines omit SV-VLA/A2C2.

column4: Remove the assumption that teleoperation or human-in-the-loop BC may treat (o_t,a_t) as a human’s instantaneous optimal response to the current observation. Human action actually responds to o_{t-Δ}; searching for the optimal Δ on wall-clock timestamps and aligning by delay should strictly improve contact-task SR under equal-token BC, making instantaneous alignment a non-load-bearing convention.
column6: There are 0 matching cracks: ACT is partial +2401.02117 is a miscitation of Mobile ALOHA. Break-glass ①② fail. A Δ grid search is a trivial mechanism; under medium overlap with latency work, the idea reaches borderline but not SA.

column4: VLA failures on instructions containing temporal conjunctions such as then/while/before are attributed to weak visual grounding. An alternative is that the action head never consumes temporal logic, only entity slots. An explicit temporal-program token should repair temporal instructions better than greater visual resolution or multiobject grounding, while both should tie on pure-entity instructions.
column6: A temporal-program token≈stage/subgoal is adjacent to existing work. The then/while/before comparison has diagnostic value but the method is below clear-accept; positioning must address Stage-Info/Confined/Altered Thoughts.

column4: Evolution: long-horizon failures concentrate at subtask boundaries mainly because the training pipeline systematically undercovers boundary states, rather than because of generic OOD or compounded error. Add a mechanism-separation arm against the inference-noise account in 2603.11642 using a 2×2 design, and define boundaries in continuous long demonstrations using explicit transition labels rather than chunk-end padding.
column6: FrameSkip/KEMO already occupy “critical-transition undercoverage and resampling.” The explicit-subtask-boundary +2×2 difference is below clear-accept; the 2603 mechanism line is misstated and the strongest baseline omits a FrameSkip-style method.

column4: Remove the assumption that a closed-loop language-conditioned VLA must reinject the complete natural-language instruction through cross-attention at every step. If first-step instruction binding and a writable progress/subgoal state match full per-step instruction conditioning on long horizons, repeated language injection is not load-bearing.
column6: The prior-work review finds medium overlap; both verified cracks are “partial”, leaving the four axiom-removal SA conditions incomplete; the compliant framing is therefore evaluated strictly. The increment over Hi Robot is thin and the baselines omit the strongest hierarchical or intermittent-language methods.

column4: Proposition: after the safety layer triggers, failure on a “second approach” is systematically higher than on the first because policy progress or memory advances along the unexecuted dangerous proposal. Rolling internal state back to a pre-trigger checkpoint should reduce second-attempt failure more than merely masking the action.
column6: No single paper fully occupies “second approach and internal rollback,” but AEGIS already reports post-filter failure. Confounding by geometric proximity and failure to pin down the architecture carrying internal state, so a mechanism-falsifiability MAJOR, leaving the idea below a clear-accept ceiling.

column4: Remove the assumption that manipulation/VLA simulation benchmarks must define evaluation support with a finite, manually enumerated table of initial configurations. If reevaluation under a continuous or procedural initial-state distribution systematically reverses SOTA, the discrete table is not load-bearing and table winners fail in the distribution tail.
column6: The prior-work review finds high overlap: the headline “fixed/discrete init-table evaluation is not load-bearing and perturbation rewrites scores and rankings” is occupied by 2606.04233/LIBERO-PRO/LIBERO-Plus. Reversal of continuous-distribution mean/p10 is only packaging, not a clear-accept-level difference. The axiom-removal SA route lacks ① because headline occupancy is nonzero; F1 novelty-dead, so Reject.

column4: Define “observation-consumption lag” τ_util: the number of steps between a detectable scene change and a significant shift in the policy’s action distribution. Policies with equal success can differ by several-fold in τ; ranking by τ should systematically reverse the mean-SR leaderboard, while training to shorten the lag should reduce τ without necessarily improving clean-start SR.
column6: τ_util has an estimand wedge relative to FASTER/RTC through a scene-transition and frozen-observation counterfactual, while the prior-work review finds medium overlap and nonzero hits. The reaction-latency neighborhood is dense, making this more a trainable diagnostic than a clear-accept proposition. The repair arm is retained, so the verdict is AwR rather than Reject.

column4: Define “instruction-binding half-life” T_bind: along an entire long trajectory under a fixed instruction, the timescale over which mutual information I(a;ℓ|o) between instruction and action distribution decays by half. A short half-life means the latter half effectively becomes an unconditional policy; rebinding mid-trajectory should repair only late failures.
column6: Relative to LangForce Information Collapse, T_bind adds an intratrajectory timescale. The prior-work review has no complete isomorph but the overlap is medium. Language-conditioning failure is already occupied, and half-life and mid-trajectory rebinding has at most a borderline ceiling below clear-accept; the verdict is AwR.

column4: Recheck: have a VLA use its own forward model to predict the expected proprioceptive or force stream, an efference copy, and re-encode expensive vision only when measured and predicted streams diverge beyond a threshold. Sensor surprise then triggers compute allocation.
column6: The adaptive-compute umbrella has medium overlap. Changing the gating signal or channel relative to ElegantVLA/AC² does not reach a clear-accept-level difference; mechanism transfer caps SA.

column4: Remove the assumption that a manipulation world model must treat object inertia, friction, and other dynamics parameters as constant within one episode. Allow online payload-parameter updates after grasping or pouring; planning on variable-payload sequences should dominate a constant-parameter WM.
column6: The axiom-removal route fails because the overlap is medium under condition ①. The cracks match, but the appearance of simply gluing SysID to a WM suppresses the clear-accept ceiling.

column4: Proposition: long-horizon failures concentrate at the interface between slow intent state and fast servo control, rather than in either layer’s individual errors. With one layer frozen, repairing only the interface should reduce boundary failures more than enlarging either layer separately.
column6: Stale or misaligned interface accounts are occupied by UniFS/Jetson-PI. The δ_iface and capacity-separation experiment adds value, but the headline difference is unlikely to support clear-accept.

column4: Remove the assumption that dense contact supervision for contact-rich BC/VLA requires dedicated force or tactile sensors. Motor-current residuals can serve as a training-time contact teacher while deployment remains vision-only; this should match a force-sensor-conditioned policy and substantially outperform no force supervision.
column6: The prior-work review finds high overlap: FD-VLA (2602.02142) already covers the headline “use force/contact signals during training and deploy without a dedicated force sensor.” The combination of a motor-current-residual teacher and vision-only removal of the force channel falls below clear-accept. Axiom-removal break-glass ①, which requires low overlap, fails; F1 CRITICAL; the verdict is Reject.

column4: The test-time gain from searching action chunks does not come from “sampling more”; it requires the scorer to estimate return under a partial closed-loop rollout. At equal budget, an open-loop video or action-likelihood scorer should not improve SR, while a closed-loop scorer should.
column6: Both sides of the mechanism are occupied by BID/τ0-WM. A closed-loop scorer adds env interaction or privileged progress, making it incomparable with an open-loop budget of equal N forward passes; the estimand is confounded, and the diagnostic form has a borderline ceiling.

column4: Negative transfer in a multitask VLA is caused not by task-semantic conflict, but by one shared action head serving incompatible commit-horizons: short-horizon contact vs long-horizon transport. Routing by optimal commit-horizon should reduce negative transfer better than a task-ID MoE, while changing horizon labels within one task should induce false negative transfer.
column6: The package combining the proposition “commit horizon is the primary cause,” H* bucketing, and a false-negative-transfer arm has no one-paper isomorph, but components are dense on both DEHP×DyGRO/HiMoE sides. H* is easily collinear with task family, making clear-accept unstable and capping novelty.

column4: Proposition: the main cause of long-horizon world-model planning failure is not stepwise pixel or latent drift, but indistinguishable goal satisfiability in latent space after multiple steps—equal prediction error but opposite reachability. A goal-satisfiability contrastive head should improve planning more than further reducing multistep reconstruction error.
column6: Prescreening found direct occupancy: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.07278.

column4: Evolution: make total energy a first-class embodied evaluation quantity. Promote the energy–recovery tension to a decisive attribution arm, and either align the simulation energy proxy with real edge joules or explicitly narrow the conclusion. Test whether rankings reverse under a fixed energy budget and whether energy-optimal policies systematically sacrifice long-tail recovery.
column6: Adding energy to evaluation is occupied by Price/BEHAVIOR (medium overlap), and the 2×2 recovery-arm difference is not established at clear-accept, so novelty is capped. The pure diagnostic has no repair arm and no dimension reaches 8+; the verdict is borderline. The minimal falsification is executable and falsifiable on 1×H100, so the idea does not warrant rejection.

column4: Remove the assumption that manipulation-policy action spaces must emit nontrivial dense control every step and cannot treat holding or waiting as first-class primitives. Promote wait/hold to the same level as move. Proposition: many contact failures arise because the policy moves when it should not; an explicit wait-for-settling primitive should reduce peak force and slip failures.
column6: The axiom-removal form is valid, but break-glass does not apply: ① medium overlap rather than low and ② only 1 crack “matches,” with the other partial, closing the SA route. The intermittent-control, AWE, and tempo neighborhood is crowded, and the difference is below clear-accept. A falsification with placebo arms can kill the wager; the verdict is AwR.

column4: Proposition: the disadvantage of a continuous action head relative to a discrete tokenizer in precision-fit tasks is caused primarily by defining loss in absolute pose error rather than assembly-constraint residual. Moving the BC loss into measurable constraint-residual space should close the gap, while a continuous head with an equal quantization-noise floor should still trail the residual-loss head.
column6: The competing explanation and residual-loss arm are viable, but pose-guided assembly residual supervision and tokenizer denoising/compression are close neighbors (medium overlap). The 2×2 difference is not established at clear-accept, so novelty is capped. Falsification is executable on 1×H100; SA is unavailable.

column4: Define “cross-dataset label-clock bias” δ_clk: differences in timestamp semantics such as command time vs actuation time. The bias should predict contact-phase negative transfer during multisource co-training; aligning all sources to actuation time should repair it better than normalizing action coordinates.
column6: The prior-work review finds medium overlap because Green-VLA already aligns cross-source frequency/speed and retargeting. The xcorr lag cannot identify the semantics “command/actuation/arrival,” and a pure injection arm is nearly a relabeling tautology, so 2 MAJOR findings cap the result. A repair arm and executable falsification prevent rejection.

column4: Define “language temporal-grounding error” τ_lang: the difference between the timescale implied by manner or duration instructions and policy execution time. Duration-instruction failure should be driven by τ rather than spatial grounding, and an explicit duration head should improve only the duration subset.
column6: The prior-work review finds medium overlap: ReMem already includes hold/press-for-N-sec temporal memory. A duration head is an established mechanism; an insufficient clear-accept difference and baselines that omit ReMem/progress produce 2 MAJOR findings. An orthogonal subset makes the idea falsifiable, so the idea is not rejected.

column4: Define “local instability of the success detector” σ_succ: the detector’s flip rate under ε-perturbations around successful terminal states. Reported success is inflated for high-σ tasks or policies; detector smoothing or terminal-state thickening should improve worst-case success only in the high-σ subset and reverse model selection.
column6: The prior-work review finds medium overlap, with 2606.03134 false-success and VLM-SD visual perturbation. Physical ε perturbations mix true task margin with detector flips, an estimand issue, and the F1 increment is thin, producing 2 MAJOR findings. A selective repair arm remains, so the idea is not rejected.

column4: Define “environment-windfall success rate” ρ_windfall: the share of reported successes that disappear after freezing rebound or resampling contact noise. Current metrics count environmental gifts as policy skill. Rankings by forced success should reverse the leaderboard, while windfall-downweighted training should improve only forced success.
column6: The ρ_windfall estimand is misaligned with “environmental gifts” because forced dynamics also remove controllable rebound skills. The neighborhood has medium overlap and is unlikely to reach clear-accept; the repair arm supports AwR.

column4: Define “corpus-era isotope bias” δ_era: cross-era transfer degradation caused by collection period, firmware, or camera ISP changes under the same embodiment and task. Scaling curves mistake era drift for data gain.
column6: The real claim concerns fleet firmware/ISP era isotopes, but the minimal falsification permits synthetic ISP or latency, whose signal cannot falsify the headline. The scaling-variable critique is valuable, but medium novelty caps the result.

column4: Define “infeasible-instruction confident-attempt rate” ρ_overcommit: when the scene makes an instruction physically infeasible, the policy still emits a full low-entropy attempt trajectory. Standard feasible-only evaluation never reports this quantity; adding infeasibility probes should reverse method rankings.
column6: The prior-work review finds high overlap: RationalVLA, RAMA, and OBEYED already occupy the headline protocol for rejecting infeasible instructions and over-grasping. The combination of naming ρ and reversing the feasible leaderboard falls below a clear-accept-level difference.

column4: Remove the assumption that a multiview manipulation VLA must synchronously encode every camera at every control step. Encode only the view with the greatest change or information gain and the fast proprioceptive channel; at equal FLOPs, it should match full per-step multiview fusion with lower latency.
column6: The prior-work review finds high overlap: BFA++ (2602.20566)/See-Selectively substantially occupy the headline “multiview processing need not be exhaustive every step.” The residual difference—skip an entire encode path vs encode everything before token pruning and an equal-FLOPs three-arm protocol—is below clear-accept. The axiom-removal route fails because overlap≠low and only 1 crack matches +1 is partial, fewer than ≥2 matches.

column4: Proposition: early saturation in RL post-training of a frozen VLA is caused not by sparse rewards, but by free-space steps dominating gradients and drowning contact phases. Phase-gated RL that backpropagates only through contact-event windows should continue improving while full-trajectory RL plateaus.
column6: The headline cause is free-space gradients drowning contact, with no isomorphic prior-work hit and a four-arm falsification that can kill it. However, the MAJOR finding under F1 is that the method itself is classic phase/option-gated RL in a setting occupied by DSRL/RIPT. Whether the headline supports clear-accept depends on accepting a “non-trivial reinterpretation”; under a strict reading it misses the SA bar.

column4: Proposition: long-horizon action-chunk failures are caused not by action-content error, but by absolute time indices losing synchronization with environmental event phase. Output “phase relative to the next contact/release event∈[0,1]×local action”; it should improve long-horizon tasks and tie on short-horizon tasks.
column6: The combination of the proposition “long-horizon degradation arises from absolute-time/event-phase desynchronization” and event-relative phase as an output coordinate has no isomorphic prior work. However, the MAJOR finding under F1 is that PACE/DVAC/RTC/stage interfaces densely occupy the phase×chunk neighborhood. Relative to PACE, changing the output axis vs changing execution length is borderline and unlikely to sustain clear-accept.

column4: Recheck: move capacity from parameters into state—a VLA with extremely small weights and a large writable recurrent state becomes stronger at deployment through state accumulation without gradients.
column6: CRITICAL F1: the prior-work review finds high overlap. AURA already combines a frozen backbone and writable recurrent deployment state vs KV without backbone gradients. “Extremely small weights and large state” is not a clear-accept-level difference.

column4: Remove the assumption that correction or intervention data must be produced synchronously by a human inside the robot’s real-time control loop. A human watches open-loop replay and marks asynchronous correction segments on the timeline; if recovery-task success matches synchronous HIL takeover at equal annotation time, real-time synchronization is not load-bearing.
column6: The axiom-removal SA route fails because the overlap is medium rather than low, 0 cracks match, and two are only partial. On the ordinary scale there is 1×MAJOR (F1 residual vs offline DAgger/HG-DAgger). Falsification is executable, so the verdict is AwR rather than SA.

column4: Proposition: a VLA treats a runtime natural-language correction such as “lighter” or “move left” as a new task requiring complete rebinding, rather than as local modulation of the current skill. A local-modulation head should improve only correction scenarios, while full reconditioning should not.
column6: CRITICAL F1: the prior-work review finds high overlap. YAY uses full language reconditioning, while LILAC uses low-dimensional modulation from short corrections; both arms already exist. Packaging a VLA differential head is insufficient for clear-accept separation.

column4: Evolution: make total energy a first-class embodied evaluation quantity by promoting the energy–recovery tension to a decisive 2×2 arm. Narrow the conclusion to a proxy domain already calibrated against an edge power meter; under a fixed joule budget, SR ranking reversal and a recovery arm are the survival signal.
column6: The energy-as-KPI literature is medium (2508.06295/PEGrad/CEGB/GovBench). The 2×2 joint survival signal, comprising ranking reversal and recovery, is falsifiable and the search is sufficient. However, the headline is an evaluation protocol assembled from known axes, with no repair arm, and its pure diagnostic/change-of-evaluation form is capped at borderline; the difference is unlikely to sustain clear-accept.

column4: Remove the assumption that a manipulation world model used for planning must use dense stepwise next-observation or next-latent regression as its primary training objective. If a sparse contact-event graph and terminal reachability classifier matches MPC using stepwise latent MSE under the same planning budget, stepwise regression is not load-bearing.
column6: The axiom-removal route closes because 0 cracks match: 1911.00371 is a mismatch, and I-JEPA is only partial. The overlap is medium with EV-WM/Sparse ND/contact WM. Replacing the primary loss creates a discriminating comparison, and the falsification is executable, but in ordinary form novelty remains below clear-accept.

column4: Define “demonstration preview residual” δ_preview: teleoperated a_t targets future state o_{t+δ}, not an instantaneous response to o_t. Instantaneously aligned BC fits preview as noise; realignment by δ should improve contact SR only in the high-preview subset.
column6: The combination of δ_preview as a first-class estimand and heterogeneous repair across high/low subsets has no direct prior-work hit, but it faces strong medium duality risk with the DA-DP/delay-realignment family. The repair arm and falsification are clear; the verdict is AwR and SA is unavailable.

column4: Evolution: diversity in interactive-WM imagined trajectories measures noise width, not reachable-set coverage. Use reproducible particle/grid reachable-set IoU as the training and selection target; aligning coverage should improve planning, while increasing noise diversity should not.
column6: Novelty is capped by the medium-overlap IDM/BIRD/LEXA cluster (F1 MAJOR); the repair arm and placebo are viable, but the gap from clear-accept is insufficient for SA.

column4: Remove the assumption that online collision/over-force gating for contact-rich VLA deployment must read external wrist or joint F/T hardware: if a motor-current and kinematic-residual monitor matches the miss rate and task SR of F/T gating under the same false-positive budget, external F/T is not load-bearing.
column6: CRITICAL F1, high overlap; sensorless CD and TA-VLA already show that external F/T is not a hard prerequisite, and only 1 crack item fully agrees.

column4: In shared autonomy, takeover value is driven primarily by lead time τ_lead relative to the point of irreversibility, rather than the quality of the post-intervention corrective trajectory; optimizing earlier prompts or warnings alone should reduce irreversible failures more than distilling corrective actions.
column6: The τ_lead estimand and placebo are discriminative, but the timing literature is dense (Thrifty/AIM/ToR), so novelty is capped (F1 MAJOR), leaving insufficient support for SA.

column4: Remove the assumption that multi-embodiment VLA co-training requires explicit morphology alignment in action space (unified EE/IK retargeting/shared action head): if per-embodiment action heads with zero alignment layers match an explicit-alignment pipeline under the same data budget, morphology alignment is not load-bearing.
column6: Prescreening found a direct occupant: https://arxiv.org/abs/2408.11812.

column4: Revisit: name and measure the "action collapse" pathology in latent-dynamics world models, then add an action-sensitivity contrastive loss weighted by local data controllability so the WM assigns capacity to controllable dimensions and recovers evaluability and controllability.
column6: CRITICAL F1: the prior-work review finds high overlap; Delta-JEPA (2606.31232) already owns the headline of "action insensitivity/collapse, with an action-sensitive objective improving planning." Local-controllability weighting and a collapse-index map is only a variant-level difference, not enough for clear-accept; the F3 MAJOR finding is that the strongest baselines should include LDAD/Delta-JEPA, rather than only Empowerment/MI.

column4: Name the "false-safe silence rate" ρ_mute: the fraction of frames where the policy has high internal uncertainty or disagreement yet emits a low-entropy conservative hold or micro-motion. Standard SR entirely misses silence by treating it as a precursor to safe success; reranking by ρ_mute should reverse "robust" model selection, while an anti-silence exploration arm should reduce only ρ without necessarily improving clean-start SR.
column6: ρ_mute=(high-disagreement/dropout∧low-entropy hold) frame fraction, Kendall ranking reversals relative to SR, and an anti-silence arm form a separable estimand. Prior-work overlap is medium and no isomorphic formalization was found; the F1 MAJOR finding is that FRP/Eval-Actions/SAFE separately cover "safe freezing/hesitation/failure detection," so the margin over accept is unproven and the ceiling is borderline.

column4: Hypothesis: interactive-WM planning fails primarily because counterfactual action branches are inseparable in latent space despite equal reconstruction error and opposite reachability, rather than because of stepwise transition MSE. A branch-separability contrastive head should improve planning success more than further reducing multistep reconstruction error.
column6: CRITICAL F1: the prior-work review finds high overlap; Delta-JEPA already states that "counterfactual action branches are latent-inseparable and cause planning failure" and improves planning with action-separability supervision, while Operator-on-F establishes the limitation of stepwise MSE. The combination of a success/fail contrastive head and a frozen-reconstruction-budget protocol is a same-axis variant, insufficient for clear-accept.

column4: Remove the assumption that scaled manipulation policies require a diffusion/flow/CVAE or other generative action head to absorb multimodal demonstrations: under matched data and backbones, if a deterministic multistep residual action head matches the official generative head on genuinely multimodal tasks, the generative head is not load-bearing.
column6: The headline that generative action heads are unnecessary for multimodal demonstrations and deterministic iterative regression can match flow is directly covered at paper scale by 2512.01809 Much Ado across 28 benchmarks including VLA fine-tuning, with high overlap. Delete-assumption break-glass condition ①, zero headline matches, is unmet; the residual of purely deterministic training and edge-latency forcing is not a clear-accept-level difference. F1 has no novelty and is CRITICAL.

column4: In-distribution VLA instruction-following success is caused by language strings acting as task-cluster/data-shard indices, not by compositional semantic understanding: paraphrases shuffled within a cluster should cause almost no drop, while labels swapped across clusters should collapse performance.
column6: The headline, ID instruction following≠compositional semantics and language acts as a shard-index or task identifier, is explicitly covered by DeMiAn §2.1 and the evidence bundle "LIBERO Q4 language embeddings≈task-ID and 2512.06951 task-embedding replacement," yielding a direct high overlap match. Its key prediction of a within-cluster shuffle drop <5pp also contradicts the 22–52pp drops in LIBERO-Para; the 2×2 would likely reproduce known results. F1 has no novelty and is CRITICAL.

column4: The environment-count–generalization power law in imitation learning is driven by coverage of contact modes and constraint types, not visual-appearance diversity: many skins with the same contact geometry should barely improve OOD performance, while few skins spanning many contact modes should absorb the power-law gain.
column6: This explanation of a recognized phenomenon, contact-mode coverage rather than visual appearance as the causal scaling axis, has medium overlap with no headline occupant, and a minimal falsification experiment in simulation on 1×H100 is executable and decisive. However, the discriminating OOD experiment combines new appearance∧new tolerance, partially baking in the |C| power law and misaligning the estimand with the headline (MAJOR); appearance-only and tolerance-only tests are required for clean support, so the clear-accept probability is unestablished and the ceiling is borderline.

column4: The unexamined quasi-static assumption that momentum is negligible across the data, action representation, and evaluation of general manipulation VLAs excludes an entire class of momentum-driven tasks. Removing that bias with momentum-aware representation and high-frequency closed-loop control should substantially outperform quasi-static VLAs on dynamic tasks; no gap would falsify the load-bearing bet.
column6: The scaffold of dynamic data, high-frequency closed-loop control, and dynamic benchmarks for π0/OpenVLA is occupied by DynamicVLA/DOMINO/F2F-AP, while LeVERB already identifies the quasi-static assumption. The overlap is medium and only 1 crack item agrees, leaving break-glass conditions ① and ② unmet; momentum tasks and impulse representation provide a real delta, but the ceiling is borderline.

column4: Visual observations of deformable objects, cables, and granular matter are many-to-one projections of internal state, including hidden layering, internal stress, and subsurface configurations, so Markov visual policies are fundamentally underdetermined on these tasks. Failures should concentrate in states with high observation-aliasing rates, and one active disambiguation micro-action should repair them better than higher visual resolution or longer history.
column6: IntentVLA occupies the aliasing name, diagnosis, and benchmark, while SGTM2.0/FlingBot covers active disambiguation in deformable domains, so substantial differentiation is required. The observation-equivalence classes in the aliasing-rate estimand and their confusion with expert multimodality are undefined, yielding 2 MAJOR findings and a capped score; the "active disambiguation vs increased capacity" comparison is not occupied by a single paper.

column4: Fine-tuning a VLA to improve task A can induce safety regression on untouched task B through shared low-level force/velocity priors, making its force and velocity distributions systematically more aggressive while capability-only evaluation misses the change. Distribution shift in held-out-task force/velocity should predict safety degradation independently of capability, and a force-prior anchor should repair it selectively.
column6: Mechanism-transfer condition ②, nontrivial adaptation, is unmet because PAC-ACT already provides an in-domain force anchor and peak-force-tail metric and LLM-domain anchor regularization is established. The phenomenon itself is a pure bet, and LIBERO/RoboCasa lack ready-made high-force fine-tuning subsets and fragile-damage events; constructing them counts as a MAJOR finding. The longitudinal causal design differs genuinely from static cross-evaluation, but all components exist and only the combination is unoccupied.

column4: Revisit the transfer of machine-oriented video coding to robot corpora: allocate lossy-compression bitrate by policy relevance and test whether fleet-scale data storage can be reduced by an order of magnitude without degrading policy performance, with only duplicate checking added before unchanged reevaluation.
column6: The revival condition is satisfied after reading 12 papers, but duplicate checking shows that Robo-DM already covers the result side with uniform 70–75x compression and no success loss, while the nearest mechanism, saliency allocation for detection, saves only another 29%, far below the expected ≥3x; novelty ceiling and value ceiling yield two MAJOR findings. The combination has no exact hit and the falsification experiment is cheap and decisive, making this near-AwR.

column4: Remove the load-bearing assumption that tactile signals must be factory-calibrated to physical units before fleet co-training: use contact events as cross-unit invariant anchors for a self-calibrated relative-signal representation. If it matches a calibrated pipeline across skin-instance replacement and drift, calibration is not load-bearing; a substantial deficit falsifies the wager.
column6: Break-glass condition ① is unmet because the overlap is medium: AnySkin/TactX already demonstrates calibration-free cross-instance co-training, deleting the result side of the target assumption, so evaluate on the ordinary scale. The contact-event anchoring mechanism has no hit, 2/2 crack checks agree, and the experiment is cheap and decisive, but headline occupancy and experimental-platform confounding produce two MAJOR findings and cap the result at AwR.

column4: Domain randomization independently randomizes visual and dynamics channels, thereby destroying the natural correlation from appearance to physical properties and preventing policies from inferring mass or friction visually. Correlation-preserving joint randomization should beat independent DR only on tasks that require visual inference of physics, while anti-correlated DR should perform worst.
column6: The core empirical prediction, visually conditioned physics inference substantially outperforming blind DR, is occupied by Phys2Real; the repair arm, correlation preservation, requires a real joint distribution and is not realistically deployable. The residual narrows to a theoretical account and three-arm attribution, yielding two MAJOR findings and an AwR ceiling. The three-arm design aligns with the estimand and is executable on 1xH100 hardware.

column4: Revisit: measure the action information rate actually required by a task in bits/s, test whether action decoding can use almost zero parameters, and place nearly all tiny-VLA capacity in perception, with only duplicate checking added before unchanged reevaluation.
column6: The estimand MAJOR remains: the headline says "the information rate actually required by the task," while the measurement captures expert-trajectory imitation information. Duplicate checking is now substantive, but three neighboring lines narrow the remaining gap: 2502.08623 measures H(A|S), TinyVLA shows a small action head, and Fitts/10bits-s occupies the intuition. The difference is insufficient for clear-accept, so novelty is capped and SA is unavailable.

column4: Remove the assumption that the object of embodied evaluation is a frozen checkpoint's one-shot success rate.
