Deployment buys a self-improving system with data feedback; policies with similar frozen SR should have improvement-slope rankings under a fixed feedback protocol that diverge from frozen rankings, so evaluation should report improvement per unit of deployment experience.
column6: The proposition is occupied by the Pacchiardi 2026 position that trajectories rather than snapshots should be evaluated and by HoloMind IR, leaving only an embodied protocol instantiation and capping novelty. Improvement-slope ranking depends on the feedback recipe and lacks a robust arm (MAJOR); break-glass condition ① has medium overlap and condition ② has only 1 agreeing crack item, so both are unmet and SA is unavailable.

column4: Standardized exogenous perturbations in robustness evaluation do not measure deployment recovery: deployment failures often grow from trouble states created by the policy itself. Robustness rankings under exogenous perturbations should diverge from rankings for recovery from self-induced failures, and a self-induced-state perturbation curriculum should repair only the latter.
column6: The same proposition is published in the GUI domain by 2605.29447, and manipulation-domain components are occupied by SO-101/FRBench/FailureBench; a mechanism transfer with a nonzero target-domain match is ineligible for SA and caps novelty. The expected Spearman<0.5 signal is inseparable from the noise floor, with no ranking-reliability arm (MAJOR).

column4: Remove the assumption that manipulation perception and prediction must use the "object" as their basic unit. Objects are the right abstraction only on rigid tabletop benchmarks; contact-patch representations should outperform them on deformable and granular tasks, and failure to do so would confirm that objects are load-bearing.
column6: The headline is stated as an accepted conclusion in a Science Robotics 2025 review (DOI 10.1126/scirobotics.adt1497) and EC-Flow (2507.06224) measures a +45% gain for non-object units on deformable tasks, indicating high overlap and a direct hit (CRITICAL/F1). Both crack items fail verification: 1810.01566 DPI-Net retains object-level abstraction through hierarchical modeling, while 2209.14860 DINOSAUR concludes that slots are viable on real data, the opposite direction. Treating form 5 as rhetoric-compliant and scoring it ordinarily leaves only a controlled comparison for an established consensus.

column4: The transfer gain from sim pretraining is caused by optimization-side initialization, not the fidelity of simulated physics or visual content: an absurd sim with scrambled physics but matched statistical moments should retain most of the gain.
column6: No headline occupant was found in robotics for sim2real dynamics, and the minimal falsification experiment (A faithful sim / B moment-matched scrambled sim / C training from scratch, 1×H100 for about 4 days) is cheap and decisive. However, 2008.11687 already makes the isomorphic causal argument in visual classification by destroying content, preserving low-order statistics, measuring residual gains, and attributing them to optimization; 2502.19249 implements the moment-matching control in the LLM domain and reaches the opposite conclusion (MAJOR). Duplicate checking is also weak exactly in the target-domain slice: Semantic Scholar returned zero results throughout due to 429, recent 6 months of sim2real/VLA pretraining ablations were not covered, OpenHLM remains unverified, and non-paper occupants were not checked (MAJOR). With ≥2 MAJOR findings, no dimension at 8+, and no bound repair arm, the ceiling is borderline.

column4: The apparent advantage of EE-space actions over joint-space actions is caused by a mismatch between L2 loss and the Jacobian condition number in joint space, not task-aligned semantics: a Jacobian-metric loss should let the joint-space head match the EE head.
column6: The claimed effect is contradicted by the submission's own strongest baseline: Demystifying (2602.23408) reports joint delta 95.9% vs EE delta 91.4% for single-arm DP and 97.8% vs 92.2% for multitask DP. The global winner is joint and chunk-wise delta, not the claimed EE-delta, with EE leading only in cross-embodiment transfer. Thus the accepted phenomenon that EE substantially beats joints runs opposite to the nearest large ablation, so the predicted first step, reproducing B behind A by 8–15pp, is unavailable; the causal question's premise fails and cannot be repaired within this version (CRITICAL/F6). Another MAJOR: MimicIK (2606.15148) already implements the discriminating arm through an equivalent FK consistency loss, while duplicate checking remains incomplete because S2 returned 429 throughout, Peters & Schaal was not read in full, and Jacobian-weighted loss was not checked in LeRobot/Octo/OpenVLA.

column4: Remove the task-agnostic low-level balance controller from a humanoid stack: balance is not an invariant that must be outsourced, but a torque resource the task policy can spend, so balance reserves rather than policy capability cap payload in heavy-load manipulation.
column6: The proposal has the delete-assumption form, all five fields, a cheap and decisive falsification experiment with a reasonable shape constraint that the gap widens monotonically with torque saturation, and substantive duplicate checking across 11 papers. However, the prior-work review finds medium overlap, while delete-assumption exception ① requires low overlap and is unmet, so SA is capped. The payload ceiling also has an unexcluded competing explanation: FALCON Appendix A.6 attributes the reported 2 kg per-arm limit to motor thermal failure, while its monolithic baseline gives the opposite conclusion that decoupling does not impair force capacity. The sim experiment omits thermal effects and cannot support the real-world attribution, adding 1 MAJOR and leaving an accept-w-rev verdict.

column4: Published "data scaling exponents" for robot imitation learning are systematically underestimated because doubling data under a fixed epoch count puts every measurement in the undertrained regime: data is not the sole axis, and optimal training compute grows superlinearly with data.
column6: Independent duplicate checking directly falsifies the headline premise. The named target, 2410.18647(Appendix C), adjusts epoch count inversely with data volume until convergence, from 800 on the smallest set to 75 epochs on the largest, implying C∝D^0.5, rather than the claimed "fixed epoch⇒linear compute." Its published exponent is therefore not suppressed by undertraining (CRITICAL false premise). The causal account is also a textbook Chinchilla transfer from LLM-to-robot transfer, while adjacent embodied IL work (Waymo 2506.08228) already measures a compute-optimal frontier with closed-loop evaluation, eliminating novelty as well.

column4: In continual deployment, intervention rates decline over time primarily because supervisors adapt by avoiding known failure modes, not because the policy improves: a frozen-policy arm should exhibit a decline of comparable magnitude.
column6: The frozen arm, performance-slope ratio, and identification device that separates machine/human learning are mature in the BCI co-adaptation literature, with Abu-Rmileh 2019 running the full design and 2606.20569 giving identification conditions. The same causal account, "intervention decline reflects a human-side learning curve," is published in remote driving by 2504.00246, so this is a cross-domain transfer of method and claim rather than a new mechanism, and the overlap is medium, which caps SA. The falsification experiment is also an N=16×5 human study with a deceptive interface that falsely claims to update from corrections, requiring IRB approval for deception and new recruitment (~53 person-hours) and creating material execution risk for one researcher on a short lifecycle. These two MAJOR findings leave accept-w-rev.

column4: Remove the assumption that BC must copy the absolute contact or grip forces in teleoperated demonstrations. Decompose demonstration force into the task-required lower bound and an operator safety surplus and learn only the lower bound; failures on fragile or precision tasks should be driven by inherited overgrip surplus rather than perception.
column6: The result-side headline, learning the minimum sufficient force for fragile objects without losing performance, is occupied by Gentle Grasping (2409.10371) and classical minimum-force/stress-minimizing grasping. The surviving novelty is limited to teleoperation-overgrip debiasing and failure attribution. Delete-assumption channel ① has medium overlap rather than low, while under condition ②, only 1/2 of the crack items agree because RETAF does not support the overgrip claim; both fail and SA is unavailable. The minimal falsification experiment is pure sim, requires synthetic overgrip injection, and is nearly self-confirming, so its external validity is doubtful.

column4: Failures among a fleet sharing one set of weights are correlated: one blind spot can disable many robots simultaneously, so the fleet-availability tail is determined by failure correlation ρ_corr rather than mean single-robot SR. Name ρ_corr; input jitter or heterogeneous checkpoints should reduce ρ_corr with almost no change in single-robot SR.
column6: The failure-correlation estimand conditional on marginal SR is established in ensemble ML by 2502.03937 using ρ and in FL by 2604.16090, while the causal account that correlated failures govern availability tails and diversity decorrelates them is published in reliability engineering, cloud systems, and FL. Only the near transfer to policy fleets remains. Mechanism-transfer exception ① fails immediately because 2603.11400 acknowledges a nonzero target-domain match, so SA is unavailable. The minimal experiment treats 64 noise seeds in one sim as a fleet; with shared weights, ρ_corr≈0 is unlikely to trigger, leaving only the signal that decorrelation preserves SR and failing to represent real fleet heterogeneity.

column4: Manipulation world models divide the future into controllable and exogenous parts but omit a third class: ballistic aftereffects that, once initiated by an action, evolve autonomously to a terminal state and cannot be further controlled. Planning failures should concentrate in these segments; the WM should predict their terminal states rather than each frame, and identifying them should matter more to planning success than overall framewise accuracy.
column6: Framewise error≠control success is established globally by 2607.10362; terminal-state jump prediction exists in SKIP 2606.00664/Gumbsch 2206.02042/EV-WM 2606.13053; and the controllable/exogenous split exists in 2605.01694/FlowMo 2606.13817. Novelty is limited to segmenting a third class by action controllability and localized attribution. The prior-work review finds medium overlap, and every component has close published neighbors, so no single-paper difference reaches clear-accept and rule16 caps the result below SA.

column4: Remove the load-bearing assumption that a policy or world model may treat forward kinematics from joint encoders as true end-effector pose. On low-cost compliant arms, FK has load-dependent systematic bias; estimating true pose as a latent variable should eliminate most contact-approach failures and cross-unit transfer failures.
column6: Crack evidence 2312.03673 fails verification and triggers rhetoric compliance; on the ordinary scale, the unverified bet and the sim-real attribution gap produce 2 MAJOR findings and cap the score. The prior-work overlap is low and the falsification experiment includes a zero-compliance attribution arm, clearing the Reject threshold.

column4: DSRL steering of a frozen VLA in noise space beats action-space RL because noise-space actions naturally remain on the support manifold of the flow model, not because the optimization geometry is smoother. Projecting action-space RL back onto the same manifold should collapse the advantage.
column6: The headline causal account "noise-space advantage comes from manifold alignment" is explicit across LP-DS/ReFORM/LPS/DPPO, and LP-DS directly contrasts noise with action space and identifies DSRL off-manifold degradation. Only a confirmatory ablation remains, which cannot be defended within the lifecycle (F1 counts as CRITICAL). Proposition-occupancy checking also finds that nobody actually advances the opposing "smoother optimization geometry" hypothesis; DSRL attributes its gains to avoiding BPTT and sample efficiency, so the target is a strawman and counts as MAJOR. A pure diagnosis without a repair arm has no dimension at 8+, capping it at borderline (MAJOR).

column4: Deployment catastrophes are not single high-uncertainty actions, but avalanches of high-confidence actions after an initial silent error. Stepwise uncertainty cannot see the avalanche; a trajectory-consistency monitor asking whether the current state matches the state that past actions should have produced can detect it before stepwise entropy.
column6: The consistency-monitor mechanism is occupied by 2503.02552, while the minimal experiment cannot measure the prevalence claim that catastrophes are mainly avalanches, yielding 2 MAJOR findings and a capped score. The entropy-blindness estimand and attribution arm remain unoccupied, clearing the Reject threshold.

column4: Interactive world-model planning fails primarily because imagined rollouts under-cover the truly reachable set, rather than because of one-step prediction error. Calibrate the sampling head by reachable-set coverage to repair MPC on multimodal tasks.
column6: Substantive duplicate checking across 8 read papers with identifier self-checks, a single-solution/multisolution differential falsification, and a bound coverage-calibration repair arm clears the rejection threshold. However, both halves of the headline are preoccupied: 2607.10362 shows planner-reachable fidelity outperforming one-step error, while MoP-JEPA links missed branches cause multisolution failure that recovery resolves. The overlap is medium and no dimension is ≥7, so the difference does not reach clear-accept, capping novelty and excluding SA.

column4: Proprioception is not necessary for policy success but a causal-confusion shortcut. Removing or masking it should force visual servoing and improve visual OOD success, with gains increasing under pose shifts but reversing on occluded tasks that require blind operation.
column6: The headline that removing or masking proprioception improves visual OOD performance and proprioception acts as a causal-confusion shortcut is directly published by State-free Policy (2509.18644) for the same π0/ACT/DP target class, while 2602.12032 occupies the occlusion-reversal discriminator. The high overlap constitutes a direct hit; delete-assumption break-glass condition ①, a zero-hit headline with low overlap, is unmet, and direct hits receive no exemption; the verdict is Reject.

column4: Benchmark success rate is uncorrelated with irreversible-error rate because benchmark tasks are mostly resettable and forgiving, so it misses the deployment objective of avoiding irreversible errors. Adding irreversibility should decorrelate rankings and can be paired with an irreversibility-aware training arm.
column6: The headline of SR decorrelating from an ignored deployment dimension, a new metric, and a repair arm that reduces violations without lowering SR is substantively preoccupied by SafeVLA-Bench 2606.00773/SafeManip 2605.12386 and SafeVLA 2503.03480; the residue merely relabels safety as irreversibility with Kendall τ, with no new mechanism because irreversibility penalties are occupied by Grinsztajn/Krakovna/Falco. The field is crowded, so the idea is dominated; novelty does not reach clear-accept; the verdict is Reject.

column4: VLA robustness to quantization and low precision is caused by closed-loop feedback correcting quantization error at every step, not by a low-dimensional action manifold; quantization sensitivity is determined by open-loop horizon rather than bit width itself.
column6: The second half of the headline, open-loop horizon rather than bit width or manifold dimension determines sensitivity, has a clean and feasible discriminating experiment. However, the claim that closed-loop feedback absorbs error has the same prediction as the published framework in which error accumulates over the open-loop horizon (HBVLA/SilentDrift): ΔSR grows with H↑, and only H=1 partially distinguishes them, an estimand MAJOR. Phase-adaptive precision payloads are also implemented by DyQ-VLA (2603.07904), occupying the practical payload (MAJOR). The prior-work overlap is medium, so the ceiling is AwR.

column4: Video-pretrained world models transfer to control because they learn a passive prior over "what the future looks like," not action-conditioned dynamics; action conditioning is not load-bearing during pretraining.
column6: Duplicate checking supports low overlap and the three-arm experiment fits on one GPU, but near-tied downstream results can estimate only the marginal value of correct action labels, not attribute it to a passive future prior; an explicit occupant already covers the cost-benefit case for action-free video pretraining.

column4: Action chunking gains come from temporal denoising of high-frequency teleoperation jitter, not multimodal consistency or reduced compounding error; the gains should disappear on clean data.
column6: No single paper covers the headline that chunk gains arise from teleoperation-jitter denoising and disappear on clean data, but the primary criterion is confounded: clean machine-generated data has both low jitter and low multimodality, so denoising and the challenged multimodality hypothesis both predict vanishing chunk gains on clean data, failing to distinguish the named competing hypotheses (estimand MAJOR). Moreover, 2507.09061 proves that chunking still suppresses compounding error under deterministic experts with no jitter, falsifying the central prediction that the signal is ≤2% (MAJOR). The prior-work overlap is medium, so the ceiling is AwR.

column4: VLA robustness to quantization and low precision is caused by closed-loop feedback correcting quantization error at every step, not by a low-dimensional action manifold; quantization sensitivity is determined by open-loop horizon rather than bit width itself.
column6: None of the six fully read prior-work papers advances the attacked "low-dimensional manifold" attribution, suggesting a strawman. The true competitor, DyQ-VLA's "phase-intrinsic physics," is not cleanly distinguished by an H sweep, and phase-adaptive precision is already implemented there; practical-payload medium overlap caps novelty. The diagnostic form has no dimension at 8+, so it does not reach SA.

column4: Video-pretrained world models transfer to control because they learn a passive prior over "what the future looks like," not action-conditioned dynamics; action conditioning is not load-bearing during pretraining.
column6: Duplicate checking supports low overlap and the three-arm experiment fits on one GPU, but near-tied downstream results can estimate only the marginal value of correct action labels, not attribute it to a passive future prior; an explicit occupant already covers the cost-benefit case for action-free video pretraining.

column4: Action chunking gains come from temporal denoising of high-frequency teleoperation jitter, not multimodal consistency or reduced compounding error; the gains should disappear on clean data.
column6: The causal headline contradicts the published theorem 2507.09061, where chunking yields exponential gains under deterministic experts and remains effective without jitter, so the minimal experiment will probably falsify itself. The discriminating signal, gains disappearing on clean data, is confounded with the CVAE effect in ACT and remains unisolated; medium overlap and 2607 identifier uncertainty cap novelty.

column4: Transfer gains from simulation pretraining to real manipulation are caused by optimization-side initialization, not simulated-physics or visual fidelity: an absurd sim pretraining regime with scrambled physics but matched statistical moments should retain most of the gain.
column6: This is a bottleneck-localization proposal, and the three-arm minimal falsification fits on one GPU and can falsify the necessity of fidelity. However, "moment-preserving absurd sim retains gains" tests whether low-order statistical content is sufficient, not the headline that optimization initialization is causal; moment preservation still carries transferable low-order content, producing an estimand mismatch, a MAJOR. The isomorphic causal analysis was already performed in vision by 2008.11687, and VLA fidelity attribution exists in 2603.22876; medium overlap and the pure diagnosis lacks a repair arm, and no dimension reaches 8+, so the ceiling is borderline and SA is unavailable.

column4: Training-time domain randomization or data augmentation improves VLA visual OOD robustness because it implicitly flattens the action head's Jacobian with respect to visual inputs, not because it exposes more distributions. An input-Jacobian regularizer with zero augmentation should reproduce most DR gains, making the training-time augmentation distribution non-load-bearing.
column6: This is a delete-assumption proposal with a clean, decisive, single-GPU minimal falsification experiment: heavy-DR baseline, zero-augmentation input-Jacobian regularization, and a wrong-layer placebo. But two of the four exception conditions are missing: medium overlap rather than low, and crack verification has only 1908.02729 agreeing while 1711.09404 is partial. RobustVLA (2511.01331) already applies input-Jacobian regularization to π0/OpenVLA to improve visual robustness, so the mechanism has a nonzero target-domain match, capping novelty and excluding SA.

column4: Embodied VLM-as-reward tracks task progress by implicitly reading scene-level visual change, approximately object displacement or optical-flow magnitude, rather than understanding task semantics; on tasks that decouple progress from visual change, a task-agnostic visual-change proxy should approximately match (≈) the VLM reward.
column6: The proposal explains a recognized phenomenon and binds a repair arm; it has low overlap and uses a clean progress⟂visual-change design that runs at inference scale on one GPU. However, the limitations/ablation of the specified strongest baseline RoboReward (2601.00675) were unread throughout because of HTML404/PDF limits/CAPTCHA, and the claim that the target admits optical-flow or motion-magnitude confusion remains unverified secondhand evidence. This weak duplicate check against the strongest baseline is a MAJOR finding, so the difference from that baseline lacks primary-source support, capping novelty and excluding SA.

column4: Embodied VLM-as-reward tracks task progress by implicitly reading scene-level visual change, approximately object displacement or optical-flow magnitude, rather than understanding task semantics; on tasks that decouple progress from visual change, a task-agnostic visual-change proxy should approximately match (≈) the VLM reward.
column6: MARVL/RAPL already occupy the headline phenomenon that VLM rewards are nonsemantic and exploit visual shortcuts. Matching with an optical-flow proxy is a new estimand but only refines a known phenomenon; combined with a pure-diagnosis ceiling, this caps the result at borderline. A primary-source reading of RoboReward in this round resolves the previous duplicate-checking MAJOR.

column4: Incremental fine-tuning from deployment feedback plateaus because continual updates induce loss of plasticity through dormant neurons or primacy bias, not because feedback data is insufficient. Explicit plasticity maintenance should restore the improvement slope, while simply adding more feedback data should not.
column6: The delete-assumption form is valid and the falsification experiment is decisive, so no bet MAJOR applies. However, the mechanism is a direct transfer of mature conclusions from RL/continual learning with trivial adaptation; medium overlap and RECALL is in the same arena, so break-glass condition ① and mechanism-transfer condition ② are both unmet, capping the result at AwR.

column4: Action chunking and smoothness priors are treated as universally beneficial, but on impact-driven contact tasks that require impulse or breakaway friction, such as insertion by striking, debonding, or starting an interference fit, those priors systematically erase necessary nonsmooth actions. Failures should concentrate in impact-requiring subtasks and rise monotonically with smoothing strength, while a nonsmooth action head should improve only those subtasks.
column6: The headline that chunking or smoothing harms impact tasks is occupied by 2605.19592, and the nearest neighbor for the monotonic-degradation subclaim, 2605.24433, surfaced only through WebSearch and was not read. The differentiation is insufficient for clear-accept; the design is clean and feasible, so the ceiling is AwR.

column4: Large-scale VLA/BC stacks define manipulation tasks by achieving a target terminal state, with both training loss and evaluation based on terminal success. Yet many deployment tasks are process- or rate-based, such as maintaining contact force, flow rate, or balance; their deliverable is a segment satisfying dynamical constraints, not a terminal state. Terminal supervision should fail systematically on process tasks, while process-constraint supervision should improve only process tasks and have no effect on terminal-state tasks.
column6: The delete-assumption exception lacks low overlap and ≥2 agreeing crack items, with verification yielding only 1 agreement and 1 partial. The premise that process tasks require process supervision is already occupied by extensive work such as Adaptive Wiping, capping novelty.

column4: Learned manipulation world models fail to plan under OOD actions because latent dynamics are unidentifiable along those action dimensions, not because prediction error is large: training data under-excites them, so multiple equivalent fits extrapolate in opposite directions. Quantify identifiability through action excitation, or persistent excitation; excitation-aware active collection should improve planning more than adding the same amount of data uniformly.
column6: This proposal combines a competitive causal explanation with transfer of identifiability through persistent excitation and has substantive duplicate checking across 9 papers including the target MOPO text. Differential correlation of excitation spectrum vs prediction error is falsifiable and includes an excitation-aware collection repair arm, so the Stronger dimension can reach 8; however, LeJEPA (2605.26379) already occupies identifiability×WM×planning×sampling with medium overlap, so the mechanism-transfer SA exception fails the "zero target-domain matches" condition, capping the score. A mild estimand-proxy gap also remains because "low excitation"≠"unidentifiable."

column4: Positive-demonstration imitation learning has no lower-bound guarantee on catastrophic-action probability: demonstrators systematically avoid catastrophic regions, leaving them uncovered and policy extrapolation there unconstrained. Catastrophe rate should not decrease with more demonstration data, unlike ordinary failure rate, so explicit negative knowledge is required; additional positive demonstrations should not reduce catastrophe rate, while a small number of negative constraints should.
column6: Published consensus holds that positive demonstrations omit systematically avoided catastrophes and therefore require negative knowledge, and SAFE-GIL/Beyond-Imitation/Learn-What-Not-To-Do implement it in multiple methods. The only new element, a decoupled scaling differential where catastrophe rate does not decline with N, is nearly predetermined because catastrophes are constructed as zero-coverage events and offers little surprise. The contribution is dominated by consensus, and its novelty ceiling cannot be repaired within the lifecycle, making the flaw CRITICAL.

column4: Remove the load-bearing assumption, shared by learned world models, that true dynamics are stationary within a rollout: contact friction drifts with sliding or wear, while actuator gain drifts with heat or battery level during one interaction. Part of long-horizon rollout error is physical nonstationarity modeled as stationary rather than statistical compounding, so conditioning on a drift state should remove that component while reducing reconstruction error alone should not.
column6: The headline is split between 2607.05966, which provides physical reinterpretation and a friction probe, and FlowMo-WM/DALI, which conditions on drift latent variables. The prior-work overlap is medium, and the residual claim that contact-internal physics is intrinsically nonstationary but modeled as stationary is real yet narrow. SA exception condition ① requires low overlap and is unmet; the minimal falsification experiment on one H100 is executable and decisive, so the verdict is AwR rather than Reject.

column4: Negative transfer in cross-embodiment co-training is usually attributed to shared-capacity or representation conflict. An alternative is that most of it is an optimization-interference artifact, similar to catastrophic forgetting, induced by data interleaving order: training on the same data under interleaved, blocked, or curriculum order should change the sign of negative transfer, making the repairable portion a scheduling rather than capacity problem.
column6: The competitive causal account that cross-embodiment negative transfer arises from schedule-induced optimization interference rather than capacity is published in multilingual work 2312.06134/MTRL 2503.01941; this idea transfers it to cross-embodiment robotics, with adaptation close to replacing the dataset and rerunning the sweep. No dimension reaches 8+, medium overlap, and the cross-embodiment sign-flip experiment is genuinely absent. It clears the rejection threshold but is capped at AwR.

column4: Under N:1 supervision, robots are currently queued by failure probability or uncertainty, but the marginal value of intervention is decoupled from failure probability: the robot most likely to fail may already be in an unrecoverable basin where intervention is futile. Scheduling by the success-probability increase from taking over now versus not taking over should yield more fleet output than failure-probability scheduling under the same human budget.
column6: Scheduling by intervention value rather than failure probability is documented by IDA (2409.15317) and chess (2604.14465), alongside active-learning EER and emergency triage. This idea transfers that criterion into the priority slot of Fleet-DAgger for N:1 fleet scheduling and restates the irreversible basin as the triage principle. The overlap is medium and the target-domain experiment is absent, so it clears the rejection threshold but is capped at AwR.

column4: Remove the assumption that a manipulation robot must carry cameras, whether egocentric, wrist-mounted, or body-mounted. A blind robot relying entirely on fixed infrastructure cameras can test in one shot whether egocentric vision is load-bearing by using body self-occlusion during contact tasks.
column6: This has the delete-assumption form and the overlap is low, but only 1/2 of the crack items agree: 1703.01703 is a toy domain without contact, and 1 other item is partial. Break-glass condition ② requires ≥2 agreeing items that indicate the assumption is weakening and is unmet. Duplicate checking also finds opposite evidence, with an ego ablation at 80.3% vs 26% showing a large advantage over fixed cameras; the bet therefore has a strong prior toward failure, which would merely confirm the default assumption, capping SA. A minimal falsification experiment exists, runs on 1×H100, and can kill the claim, so the result is AwR.

column4: Name the "test-time improvability ceiling": fixed-state action diversity, not verifier quality, caps best-of-N gains. Robot test-time compute should fail to scale like LLMs because actions collapse near the mode, and only diversity training rather than a stronger verifier can open the curve.
column6: The prior-work review finds medium overlap: the headline mechanism that diversity rather than the verifier caps best-of-N, collapse causes a low ceiling, and diversity opens the curve is covered by single papers in the LLM domain (2407.21787/2504.13837/2512.01127). Robot best-of-N work with verifiers and the opposing claim that the verifier is the lever are also published (2506.17811/2602.12281/2607.03751). This is a mechanism transfer with nonzero target-domain matches, so SA exception condition ① is unmet. The residual novelty, formalizing f(D,r) and claiming D is the bottleneck in robotics, is not covered by one paper, and the minimal falsification experiment is executable, yielding AwR.

column4: Hypothesis: steering of a frozen VLA plateaus because high-gain directions are fixed by the pretrained frozen Jacobian and are task-independent, not because the reachable set is low-rank, an explanation that would prescribe adding rank. Alignment predicts gains, and per-task linear reparameterization should repair the plateau better than increasing rank.
column6: The prior-work review finds low target-domain VLA-steering overlap, the competing explanation is unpublished, and both a discriminating experiment and a repair arm are present. However, the mechanism of Jacobian anisotropy and fixed high-gain axes has mature precedents in image-domain diffusion/GAN latents, and the reported duplicate check does not exhaust image-to-action transfer, leaving mechanism-transfer condition ② doubtful. No dimension confidently reaches 8+, so the proposal remains below clear-accept; it is the strongest of the three candidates.

column4: Hypothesis: steering of a frozen VLA plateaus because high-gain directions are fixed by the pretrained frozen Jacobian and are task-independent, not because the reachable set is low-rank, an explanation that would prescribe adding rank. Alignment predicts gains, and per-task linear reparameterization should repair the plateau better than increasing rank.
column6: The causal account and estimand are occupied in neighboring domains by Power Steering/2602.17881/2605.07302; rechecking confirms zero target-domain hits, but "nontrivial adaptation" remains only an assertion; mechanism-transfer condition ② is therefore unmet, capping novelty. The discriminating experiment and repair arm are both present, yielding AwR.

column4: Remove the load-bearing assumption that manipulation environments are quasi-static and passive, with objects changing only when directly acted upon. Model the autonomous dynamics of reactive or agentic elements as a third class distinct from passive exogenous and controllable effects.
column6: All five fields are present, 2 crack items agree, and the falsification experiment can kill the bet, but medium overlap leaves break-glass condition ① unmet. The "misattribution" claim is also misaligned with the measured signal because DynamicVLA's latency-based competing account is not distinguished, counting as MAJOR, so the ceiling is AwR.

column4: Remove the assumption that deployment decisions must come from one forward pass through an amortized policy network. The long-horizon plateau of large VLAs should be caused by the amortization gap rather than data or capacity; online planning over a frozen latent world model should cross the plateau.
column6: The headline "large-VLA long-horizon plateau arises from an amortization gap" cannot be tested by the 1×H100 minimal experiment, which uses a simulated latent WM and a small distilled policy and no VLA, so F6 rises to CRITICAL. The same WM head-to-head between amortization and planning was already run by 2605.08732 with the opposite result, while the amortization-gap account (2010.10670) and VLA test-time-compute account (2602.12281) are published, indicating high overlap.

column4: Evolution: the unmeasured main cause of contact-approach failure and cross-unit transfer failure on low-cost compliant arms is load-dependent systematic FK bias. First measure the bias field offline on public real-robot data as a kill gate, then close attribution with measured-field replay injection and a zero-effect rigid-arm control.
column6: The headline attribution has no occupant, but 2 MAJOR findings cap the score: measured-field replay in simulation and the rigid-arm control establishes sufficiency, not that it is the real-world "main cause"; and the pose measurement in gate 1 has an extrinsics-FK loop confounded with load-dependent estimation error, which the protocol does not specify.

column4: Remove the pipeline-wide default assumption that robot video corpora must be decoded back into RGB tensors for training. A policy or world model should consume the compressed-domain bitstream directly, using keyframes, motion vectors, and residuals; matching RGB under equal data would make decoding non-load-bearing and improve training I/O by an order of magnitude.
column6: Medium prior-work overlap caps novelty because CoViAR/CoPE-VideoLM/Robo-DM each occupies one corner and only one step remains. The delete-assumption SA channel is closed because only 1 crack item agrees; confounding from the pretrained RGB encoder and shrinkage of the evidence row each count as MAJOR.

column4: A learned-world-model rollout runs systematically faster or slower than true dynamics; name this "imagined clock bias." Planning failures should concentrate in segments with high clock bias rather than high framewise error, and temporal recalibration should improve planning success more than further reducing prediction error.
column6: The three-loop kill chain is executable and binds a recalibration repair arm, with only one MAJOR for content-time entanglement/event censoring. However, the nearest-neighbor iKCE excerpt about contact-event timing remains questionable and unresolved, so a conservative novelty cap excludes SA.

column4: Name the "tokenizer RL-fine-tunability" estimand: the IL-optimal information structure for an action tokenizer, low vocabulary redundancy and high temporal overlap as in ActionCodec, is not optimal for RL post-training. A low-redundancy spiky token distribution gives RL degraded gradients, so RL lift should be inversely correlated with IL-optimal redundancy.
column6: This names a new estimand and competitive explanation, and independent duplicate checking finds zero matches for the head that IL-optimal⇒RL degradation is inversely correlated; the arXiv search for action tokenizer AND reinforcement AND fine-tuning returned 1 irrelevant result. However, ActionCodec (2602.15397) occupies the IL-optimal information axis, while Subwords-as-Skills (2309.04459) is an opposing near neighbor where compression/temporal overlap aids RL exploration. The difference is not clear-accept and requires ≥2 RL regimes to remove confounding, so novelty is capped. The minimal falsification experiment, sweeping tokenizer redundancy×GRPO/flow-noise post-training and measuring inverse correlation with RL lift, is decisive and runs on one GPU, clearing reject but capping at AwR.

column4: When a learned world model judges policies, ranking errors are driven primarily by variance blindness from a mean-seeking objective, rather than optimism bias: policies with equal means but different outcome variances look nearly equivalent to a generative WM, causing systematic undervaluation of low-variance robust policies. Name evaluator variance-discrimination; a variance-preserving stochastic/ensemble-rollout WM should recover variance rankings.
column6: This provides a competing explanation and named variance-discrimination estimand. Independent duplicate checking finds zero matches for the headline or estimand: world model AND policy evaluation AND variance returned 1 irrelevant result, and policy selection AND risk returned 0, excluding the occupied optimism-bias headline. However, StressDream (2606.00267) shares the load-bearing premise that mean rollouts miss tails, and MSE-induced mode collapse is expected rather than automatically clear accept. It needs a bias-vs-variance decomposition and an arm excluding the possibility that StressDream steering already fixes ranking, making three reject-default passes unlikely to agree. The bound variance-preserving repair arm clears the gate but caps the result at AwR.

column4: Remove the assumption that learned or generative manipulation world models must assign equal generation or modeling budgets to every future timestep in a rollout. Use high fidelity only at decision branches and skip nonbranching frames; the long-horizon collapse of an evaluator should be caused by spreading an equal budget across decision-irrelevant frames.
column6: Direct prescreen occupants: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.18208 (Looped World Models, state-adaptive compute) / https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.15160 (DLWM, dynamic WM compute allocation) / https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.27947 (SANTS, state-adaptive scheduling) / https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.07931 (One-Token-Per-Frame). Multiple individual papers occupy the delete-assumption headline of nonuniform WM compute allocation by state complexity or decision relevance; all five delete-assumption fields are present, including a quota.

column4: Compositional OOD failure in VLA, on novel object×skill combinations, is binding failure rather than Jacobian sensitivity: smoothness regularization should not repair binding, and failure rate should grow with compositional novelty rather than perturbation magnitude.
column6: Prescreening found a direct occupant: https://arxiv.org/abs/2602.24143 (Robust Skills Brittle Grounding directly diagnoses binding failure through held-out object-region pairs dropping from 44% to 0%), along with LiLo-VLA (2602.21531) / ACT-VLA (2607.00351). A single paper already covers the compositional-binding-failure headline.

column4: When a WM self-correction pipeline such as TACO's Recognize-Imagine-Label turns real-robot failures into synthetic corrections, it systematically inherits WM blind spots: corrections are generated exactly in policy-failure regions where the WM is least accurate, making the synthetic corrections unreliable, a correction echo chamber. Name the self-consistency bias of synthetic correction data; gains should decay with local WM confidence, unlike gains from real-robot corrections.
column6: Adversarial deep checking gives medium-high overlap. The mechanism, model exploitation or model collapse in high-error regions, is a textbook named conclusion: MOPO (2005.13239) formalizes it; Uncertainty-Aware RWM (2504.16680) confidence-weights WM synthetic data on real robots, a nonzero target-domain hit; WM-DAgger (2604.11351) filters by consistency; RISE (2602.11075) acknowledges the limitation; and Self-Correction-Bench (2507.02778,LLM) is adjacent. The only unpublished residual is a thin paired comparison of confidence-stratified real-vs-synthetic examples in a failure-correction loop. Mechanism transfer is dominated, novelty is capped, and a majority of three passes reject.

column4: Evolution: for load-related FK bias in low-cost compliant arms, use external pose ground truth independent of joint encoders and multi-load calibration with known masses as the first kill gate, then close the attribution.
column6: F1 novelty-dead: the prior-work review finds high overlap. The headline mechanism, external ground truth, a load/configuration-conditioned FK bias field, and compensation, is covered by 2510.19962 and industrial compliant-arm calibration. The residual is only an ε(q,m)-conditioned formulation and a partial-correlation protocol for contact-approach failure, insufficient for a clear-accept-level difference, so CRITICAL and direct Reject.

column4: Remove the assumption that manipulation simulation benchmarks and sim pretraining must lock contact dynamics to a rigid nonpenetration solver. If method rankings on precision-insertion and sliding tasks reverse under a compliant or hydroelastic contact backend, the rigid backend is not load-bearing.
column6: The delete-assumption SA channel fails because the overlap is medium≠low. This is a pure load-bearing-component diagnosis and an underpowered k=3 Kendall estimate and an overly heavy multi-method, dual-backend training design, capping it at AwR.

column4: Multilayer stacking fails primarily because the policy never consumes a support-dependency graph, not because grasp localization is inaccurate. Name the "support-destruction increment"; an explicit support head should improve only multilayer tasks and have zero effect on single-layer tasks.
column6: CRITICAL F1, support graphs and collapse prediction already occupy the mechanism in 2406.02283/2105.14764; naming ΔSupport and packaging a single-layer zero effect provides no clear-accept difference.

column4: Remove the assumption that BC/VLA demonstrations must first be resampled onto a uniform time grid at a fixed control frequency. Direct training on native timestamps from heterogeneous frequencies, dropped frames, and variable-speed teleoperation should match a resampling pipeline; interpolated pseudo-frames around contact events, rather than "misalignment," should cause the loss.
column6: The prior-work review finds medium overlap because ISR occupies the claim that heterogeneous frequency and speed harm BC. Only 1/2 of the crack items agree, closing the delete-assumption SA channel; the baselines omit ISR. The native-timestamp claim can be salvaged to borderline.

column4: VLA language conditioning is not dense semantic fusion but a hard gate that sparsely opens or closes skill subspaces. Randomly permuting task-irrelevant tokens should leave performance nearly unchanged; a dense-fusion baseline should be permutation-sensitive, while sparse-gating repair should improve compositional OOD.
column6: The prior-work review finds high overlap because SayCan–CLASP–DASL–HiRT occupy skill-gating repairs and the "language selects skills" narrative. The residual is only a token-permutation discriminator; pure diagnosis and an occupied repair cap the result at AwR.

column4: Edge-VLA degradation is driven by phase lag from coupling replanning triggers with end-to-end control latency, not weight-quantization error. Under the same latency budget, a coarse model replanning at high frequency should systematically beat a full-precision model replanning at low frequency, reversing the precision-first default.
column6: The prior-work review finds high overlap because VLASH/RTC/vla.cpp/HiRT occupy phase lag and higher-frequency repair. The form is a bottleneck-localization probe; no single paper fully occupies the matched-budget ranking-reversal protocol, but the difference is insufficient for clear-accept.

column4: Multitask VLA/manipulation leaderboards and scaling curves treat task count or language-instruction-variant count as the diversity variable, measuring the wrong quantity. True diversity is the number of contact-constraint topology classes K_topo; after balancing topology, language-count curves should collapse, topology-count curves should steepen, and topology-coverage sampling should improve only OOD-topology SR.
column6: The K_topo estimand has an executable falsification, but medium overlap with 2507.06219/2410.18647/LIBERO-PRO blocks SA. The attributable repair gain relative to env/object-diversity sampling is also unestablished; pure diagnosis and redefining an axis defaults to borderline.

column4: Offline selection of learned world models commonly uses open-loop expert-action metrics such as FVD/NLL/stepwise MSE, which measure demonstration-trajectory fidelity, while planners query the separability of consequences on counterfactual or exploratory action branches. Expert-branch ranking should correlate with planning return much less than counterfactual-separability ranking, and selecting on the latter should improve planning SR.
column6: The prior-work review finds high overlap: the headline that WM selection measures the wrong thing and offline diagnostics for checkpoint selection is occupied by VP²/2607.01736/Operator-on-F; D_cf only operationalizes the same claim, while the primary baseline still names FVD rather than CROF, so no clear-accept path exists.

column4: Automated fleet demonstration quality scores based on smoothness, peak velocity, or duration measure operator comfort and UI habits rather than information density for BC. Under equal tokens, filtering by the quality score should underperform anti-smooth or randomly retained subsets, while filtering by an information-density proxy should reverse the ordering.
column6: A falsifiable reversal of "smoothness quality indicates BC information" has an open gap because no full same-direction coverage was found. However, RINSE/PSD publish positive results in the opposite direction, weakening the clear-accept prior, and RINSE must be the primary baseline. SA is capped, while AwR remains possible.

column4: Remove the assumption that cross-embodiment VLA co-training must consume complete RGB appearance as a load-bearing visual input. If geometry-invariant channels such as depth, normals, or optical flow match full-appearance RGB on cross-embodiment transfer, RGB appearance is not load-bearing and appearance shortcuts are the primary source of negative transfer.
column6: All five delete-assumption fields are present, the falsification runs on 1×H100, and no direct-hit occupant matches depth/normal/flow against the strongest RGB with DR in cross-embodiment VLA. However, the prior-work review finds medium overlap because DP3/PCM occupy "color-free geometry≥RGB with appearance generalization," and crack evidence records only "1 agreeing item +1 partial", so break-glass condition ② fails, as does the channel overall because condition ① has overlap≠low. Relative to DP3, the SA increment is only a setting transfer, with no dimension at 8+, so the result is AwR rather than SA.

column4: Manipulation-world-model planning fails because training weights all horizons equally, allowing near-term signals to dominate gradients and leaving distant futures inseparable in latent space, not because stepwise state error is large. A horizon-discounted or bootstrapped dynamics signal should improve long-range counterfactual separability and planning SR even if stepwise MSE stays flat or worsens, reversing the MSE-selection and planning rankings.
column6: CRITICAL F1, the headline mechanism, temporal weighting of multistep latent dynamics and using the WM for planning, is occupied by TD-MPC2 (2310.16828) λ^t multi-step joint-embedding and Contextual LWM γ^h; the prior-work review finds high overlap. The residual is only an equal-weight-gradient-dominance narrative and an experiment reversing MSE/planning selection rankings, insufficient for clear-accept. This is a diagnosis or selection-ranking probe without a new repair arm relative to the occupants, so no dimension is likely to reach 8+ with confidence.

column4: EE-pose BC fails on tight-tolerance mating tasks because its output representation couples constraint-normal and tangential directions in one Euclidean action, not because perceptual resolution is poor. Switching outputs to constraint-residual coordinates, normal gap and tangential slip, should improve only mating tasks while matching pose heads in free space.
column6: The constraint-residual BC head, free-space zero effect, and anisotropic-loss pose-head control form a complete package with no direct hit and the overlap is medium. Task-frame/relative-pose/residual RL are predecessors but not isomorphic. The falsification runs in sim on 1×H100 and can kill the claim. Still, novelty is moderate, control-theory predecessors are dense, the increment is unlikely to reach clear-accept, and no dimension is reliably 8+, so the result is AwR rather than SA.

column4: Evolution: after separating content-time entanglement with a pure temporal-warp placebo and interval-censoring protocol, temporal recalibration should still improve planning success more than reducing prediction error for the learned world model's "imagined clock bias."
column6: Imagined-clock bias and recalibration differ from iKCE/play-adequacy and have an executable falsification, but the medium overlap caps SA. Arm B incorrectly labels iKCE as an event-timing supervised loss, counting as 1 MAJOR.

column4: Remove the assumption that demonstration cleaning must be completed as a separate offline preprocessing stage before policy training. If online reweighting by per-sample contribution during training matches the offline-cleaning pipeline under equal tokens, the offline-cleaning stage is not load-bearing.
column6: The prior-work review finds high overlap: AWR/AWAC/weighted BC already reweights during training without an independent offline top-k stage. Only 1 crack item agrees; practice already occupies the headline that "offline cleaning is not load-bearing", so CRITICAL F1.

column4: The deployment decision of whether to request help and the training decision of whether to collect or label a trajectory have been treated separately, but both express the same information value V_info. One estimator should predict both help-request points and high-value collection points.
column6: No single paper occupies unified V_info and cross-task ρ, and the falsification is executable. Intervention logs≠optimal VoI creates estimand confounding and counts as 1 MAJOR; medium overlap is insufficient for clear-accept, so SA is unavailable.

column4: Remove the assumption that a closed loop must retokenize and inject static task language at every step. Encode the instruction into a writable intent slot only at episode start, then run a purely visuomotor loop; it should match per-step injection under equal data, with bandwidth and latency motivating the aggressive form.
column6: CRITICAL F1, DISC/TeNet already implement and validate execution with zero language path, showing that static instructions need not be re-tokenized at every step. The prior-work overlap is high, and an equal-backbone ablation is not a clear-accept difference. Only 1 crack item agrees, so delete-assumption SA conditions ① and ② both fail.

column4: Failures on spatial prepositions or left/right are driven by binding directional words to image coordinates rather than robot-base or task coordinates, not poor 3D geometry perception. With a randomly rotated camera and a fixed instruction, image-frame binding should reverse the action while base-frame binding remains nearly invariant; a coordinate-rebinding head should repair the failures better than deeper depth estimation.
column6: The prior-work overlap is medium: the FoR/reference-frame half of the hypothesis is occupied by RoboSpatial/COMFORT. The residual, rotating the camera to separate image-frame from base-frame binding and comparing a binding head with deeper 3D, can form a borderline diagnostic and repair paper but is insufficient for clear-accept or SA.

column4: Multicamera gains come from breaking the demonstration shortcut "customary viewpoint-to-action," not from adding more scene-information bits. One camera with training-time viewpoint randomization should approach fixed multicamera performance, while adding cameras only at test time without changing the training distribution should yield nearly zero gain.
column6: CRITICAL F1, 2510.02268 and MV-MWM publish the headline causal account "fixed viewpoint-to-action shortcut, with viewpoint diversity breaks the shortcut"; high overlap. The two sharp arms only close the experiment and do not create a clear-accept difference.

column4: Evolution: when a learned world model judges policies, ranking errors are driven primarily by variance blindness rather than optimism bias. After adding a bias-vs-variance decomposition and a StressDream exclusion arm, a variance-preserving WM should recover variance rankings and improve policy-selection SR.
column6: The mean inadequacy of WM judging is occupied by StressDream/WorldGym/MiraBench with medium overlap. The bias-vs-variance decomposition and exclusion arm are falsifiable but unlikely to produce a clear-accept increment, and no dimension reaches 8+ with confidence.

column4: Remove the assumption that human-intervention trajectories can be co-trained with expert demonstrations under the same BC objective. Takeover segments are conditional rescue policies; mixing entire segments turns rescue bias into the task policy, so mixing only correction segments or using an explicit rescue head should systematically improve performance.
column6: The delete-assumption SA channel fails because the overlap is medium and crack verification is only partial. RaC retains and amplifies recovery under the same BC objective and reports gains, opposing the prescription to discard rescue behavior; the HIL falsification data is expensive to collect.

column4: A policy absorbs jitter in a remote teleoperation link as environmental randomness and becomes stochastic, then degrades when deployed over a stable link. Training with injected link-latency jitter versus deployment with dejittering should separate this explanation.
column6: The specific claim that a stable link causes degradation has almost no search hits, but DA-DP already occupies latency-conditioned IL. The repair arm is a nearby transfer, the phenomenon lacks a published anomaly prior, and diagnosis and a neighboring fix cap the result at borderline under low overlap.

column4: Remove the assumption that hard force/torque clipping can be added after training as an orthogonal safety layer. Training without limits and hard clipping at deployment induces limit-scraping motion and chatter; joint training within the limits should systematically remove the failure mode, otherwise the assumption is load-bearing.
column6: The prior-work review finds high overlap: CaT/CBF-RL/SE-SP establish that constraints or filter layers must enter training. Hard force-torque clipping and limit-scraping are only setting refinements, not a clear-accept difference; crack evidence records "1 agreement +1 partial," eliminating the delete-assumption SA channel.

column4: Failure spikes during gradual fleet rollout are caused by distribution shift from partially completed states left by coexistence with the old policy, not weak capability in the new policy. Scheduling rollout by residual-state occupancy should outperform scheduling by traffic percentage.
column6: The prior-work review finds low overlap, and the residual-state OOD scheduling estimand is open. The MAJOR finding is that the ρ_res/residual-state detector is undefined, making the gating arm inexecutable. With a defined detector and canary semantics the idea could reach borderline, but its present ceiling is below SA.

column4: RL steering of a frozen VLA plateaus because reward support does not overlap demonstration support, causing critic extrapolation, not because plasticity is lost. Reward relabeling with matched coverage should restore lift, while adding the same number of steps should not.
column6: The explanation and repair arm are valid, but the overlap is medium because LP-DS already identifies support failure. The falsification omits LP-DS as the strongest control (F3), and relabeling is easily confounded with reward shaping in the estimand. With ≥2 MAJOR findings, the ceiling is AwR.

column4: Evolution: isolate sampling-rate aliasing artifacts in cross-embodiment negative transfer by first requiring measurable contact-spectrum aliasing in real multifrequency OXE subsets as a kill gate, then replace textbook low-pass antialiasing with per-source native-timebase encoders and a shared latent clock under kinematically matched controls, separating aliasing from embodiment kinematics.
column6: The ρ_alias×ΔSR relationship with kinematic matching leaves a residual story, but the prior-work review finds medium overlap because CrossFormer already handles multifrequency co-training while negative transfer and antialiasing are separately occupied, capping SA. The repair arm's increment over variable chunks is not established at clear-accept level.

column4: Remove the assumption that multimodal embodied data, vision×proprioception×action, must be hard-synchronized to one clock before training. If explicit timestamp conditioning and random training-time modality shifts match a hard-synchronization pipeline, hard synchronization is not load-bearing; unmodeled modality latency, rather than "lack of synchronization," causes the loss.
column6: CRITICAL F1, DAM-VLA directly removes "synchronize recording to one timestamp" and compares against a synchronized baseline, creating high headline overlap. Both crack items verify as partial rather than agreeing, eliminating the delete-assumption SA channel.

column4: The open-loop full-trajectory return ranking used by a learned world model to select actions is a different estimand from what receding-horizon closed-loop execution consumes. Rescoring candidates through simulated closed-loop replanning should reverse the open-loop ranking and improve real planning SR, while reducing stepwise prediction error should not.
column6: CRITICAL F1, World-in-World/WorldPlanner occupy simulated WM-internal rescoring and RH/MPC, while Play-Adequacy/CROF occupy the claim that stepwise error≠closed-loop utility. The residual Kendall-ranking-reversal protocol does not leave a clear-accept-level opening.

column4: Revisit: train a world model with paired rollouts coupled by common random numbers to predict the outcome difference between two actions directly, making action comparison cheaper and more accurate than absolute-future prediction.
column6: Common-random-number action comparison is occupied at planning time by 2605.04732; the Δ-WM training axis differs, but not enough for clear-accept, so medium novelty is capped.

column4: Remove the assumption that offline manipulation-policy evaluation must make every trial i.i.d. by externally resetting objects and the robot to canonical initial states between trials. If method rankings systematically reverse on continuous trial sequences that preserve physical residual state, i.i.d. reset evaluation is not load-bearing.
column6: Prior-work overlap is medium with EARL/LIBERO-PRO. Crack verification is only partial, and this pure diagnosis or evaluation change has no 8+ repair arm, capping the result at AwR.

column4: Name the "planning-query/training-support mismatch" D_query: a learned world model can fit the expert-action branch well while systematically leaving support on counterfactual branches queried by the planner. Planning failures should concentrate in states with high D_query rather than high stepwise MSE; resampling or reweighting training by the planning-query distribution should improve planning SR more than further reducing expert-branch MSE.
column6: CRITICAL F1: objective-mismatch/play-adequacy has dominant high overlap. Formalizing D_query and reweighting is not a clear-accept-level difference, and 2002.04523 already occupies the repair arm.

column4: Remove the load-bearing assumption that cross-source or cross-embodiment co-training must first retarget actions into a unified coordinate system, including unified frequency units. An embodiment-native head and shared trunk should match the retargeting pipeline under equal data.
column6: HPT/CrossFormer explicitly state and demonstrate the headline that modular heads can remove retargeting during co-training, so the prior-work review finds high overlap and F1 is CRITICAL, while an equal-token three-arm design is only a narrower experiment, not a clear-accept-level difference.

column4: Name "demonstrator-conflict density" κ_demo: action entropy across operators within the same observation neighborhood and instruction. Multisource BC degradation should be driven by κ rather than visual-domain differences, and a demonstrator-mixture head should improve only the high-κ subset.
column6: The κ_demo dual-separation package has borderline value, but DemInf/Gandhi already occupy H(a|s) and multi-human incompatibility. No net-new dimension reaches 8+, so the result cannot reach clear-accept; MAJOR×2 caps it at AwR.

column4: Name "within-chunk contact-phase drift" φ_chunk: the phase error between intended contact time and the measured force peak accumulates over time within an open-loop chunk. Force-triggered relocking should repair contact SR more effectively than merely shortening the chunk.
column6: The φ_chunk name and matched-budget comparison can form a borderline diagnostic paper, but PhaForce/RDP already occupy contact phase and within-chunk force correction, while t_intended is weakly measurable. MAJOR×2 caps the result at AwR.

column4: Remove the assumption that contact-rich manipulation must consume force or tactile input at deployment. The force channel's main value should be privileged supervision that anchors vision during training; training with force but deploying without it should match using force in both phases, otherwise deployment-time force input is load-bearing.
column6: Prescreening found a direct occupant: https://api.openalex.org/works?search=TacImag%20tactile%20imagination%20vision%20proprioception%20without%20tactile%20sensors%20deployment&per_page=2&select=id.

column4: Evolution: an alternative explanation for long-horizon failures clustering at subtask boundaries is that chunk sampling or padding systematically undertrains boundary states. Add an arm that separates this mechanism from inference-noise attribution and redefine "boundary" as a sampling-window edge rather than a semantic subtask boundary; coverage resampling should reduce boundary failures, while permuting the coverage map should not.
column6: The boundary-failure noise axis is occupied by 2603.11642 with medium overlap. The repair package—"coverage +2×2+ oversampling"—is not covered by one paper and can kill the claim, but the difference is unlikely to reach clear-accept, so novelty is capped and SA is unavailable.

column4: The primary value of deployment feedback for a frozen VLA is calibrating a test-time schedule, including replanning thresholds and chunk length, rather than updating weights. With equal feedback, updating only the table should recover most of the closed-loop gain from full fine-tuning, making weight updates non-load-bearing.
column6: Frozen models with feedback and adaptive φ are both occupied as separate axes with medium overlap. The identifiable quantity in the headline is misaligned with the experimental ΔSR-recovery-rate estimand; the equal-budget claim that only φ needs updating is borderline.

column4: Cross-embodiment negative transfer is usually attributed mainly to arm-kinematics differences, but an alternative is tool or gripper end-effector changes. Tool-topology distance should predict degradation better than arm-link distance, and a tool-conditioned head should improve only the changed-tool subset.
column6: Data Analogies (2603.06450) already establishes EE morphology as a first-class transfer axis in controlled experiments. Fixing the arm makes "tool distance≫arm distance" nearly tautological, so the falsification cannot test whether tools rather than arms are the primary cause. HPT/LAD/CEI occupy the tool-conditioned repair head, and the residual combination of topology-distance regression and crossed-grid attribution is not a clear-accept-level difference; the idea is dominated and receives a Reject verdict.

column4: Evolution: operationalize failure spikes during gradual fleet rollout as partially completed residual states, using ρ_res, the fraction of state OOD under old-policy occupancy, and an executable canary gate. Scheduling by residual-state occupancy should outperform equal traffic-percentage rollout.
column6: Operationalizing ρ_res with an occupancy canary creates a useful opening, and duplicate checking is medium rather than a direct hit. However, the OOD reference is reversed, using old occupancy rather than new-policy support, and the falsification arm is misaligned with the headline causal claim, so 2 MAJOR findings cap the result at AwR, excluding SA.

column4: Name the "judge optimism radius" R_opt: within an expert-neighboring action ball, it is the largest radius at which a WM/VLM judge still ranks truly worse actions above the expert. Planning failures should concentrate where query-point R_opt is high, and calibration that shrinks R_opt should improve planning success more than reducing stepwise prediction error.
column6: The R_opt local-ranking-reversal radius has a clear estimand and calibration arm, with medium prior-work evidence and no isomorphic radius. However, the strongest counterexample Operator-on-F and existing pairwise ranking are absent from the baselines, so the net increment is unlikely to reach clear-accept, leaving 1 MAJOR finding and an AwR verdict rather than SA.

column4: Name the "recovery support hole" η_hole: the local density of demonstrated recovery actions around a failure. Low η_hole should predict unrecoverable failures independently of detection accuracy, and targeted recovery demonstrations should improve only tasks with high η_hole gaps.
column6: Naming η_hole as orthogonal to detection creates an opening, with medium duplicate checking and no direct hit. The strongest baselines omit HELP/VBT-style recovery collection, while "unrecoverable" is not narrowed relative to SAILOR/OCR search-based recovery, so 2 MAJOR findings cap the result at AwR.

column4: Remove the assumption that offline accuracy is a sufficient statistic for selecting and accepting embodied reward models or VLM judges. Accuracy is not load-bearing; the policy-improvement slope under a fixed RL budget is the acceptance measure. If the accuracy winner ranks last in improvement slope, the accuracy threshold is falsified.
column6: The headline has high overlap with Accuracy Paradox / Barking Wrong Tree. The embodied setting and β-based selection difference is insufficient for clear-accept, and the SA break-glass path requires low overlap, which is unmet.

column4: Current manipulation evaluation normalizes success rate by a fixed episode count n, measuring the wrong deployment budget: methods that fail quickly receive more retry opportunities under a fixed wall clock. Rankings by n-normalized success rate should systematically reverse against rankings by successful completions under a wall-clock budget; report completion count under fixed wall-clock or joule budgets.
column6: Changing the evaluation target and adding a repair arm is viable. Nearest neighbors already report time-to-completion, leaving the ranking-reversal claim as only a borderline increment; without occupant checking for the repair arm, it cannot break the SA ceiling.

column4: Data "diversity" scores based on visual-embedding coverage or frame deduplication measure appearance diversity rather than decision diversity. Once appearance saturates but contact modes or conditional action entropy remain narrow, marginal gains from more data should approach zero; only decision coverage should predict the scaling margin.
column6: SCIZOR already performs joint state-action deduplication, showing visual≠action. Naming D and the two-curation ranking reversal add little, while D's operational definition is soft and counts as another MAJOR.

column4: Remove the assumption that task adaptation for a frozen or black-box VLA requires first-order backpropagation updates to differentiable weights. Under equal budgets, a zeroth-order or evolutionary strategy tuning only a thin adapter should match LoRA-Adam, making backpropagation non-load-bearing.
column6: The prior-work review finds medium overlap: MeZO/ESSA occupy ZO/ES with LoRA. A matched-forward-pass VLA falsification is executable, but the transfer is insufficiently nontrivial to reach clear-accept or SA.

column4: Remove the assumption that multi-embodiment VLA co-training requires a shared global discrete-action vocabulary or unified tokenizer. If embodiment-native continuous heads and one shared vision-language tower match co-training with a global FAST-style vocabulary, the global action vocabulary is not load-bearing.
column6: CRITICAL F1, high overlap. CrossFormer/π0/Octo/RDT already implement multi-embodiment co-training without a global discrete vocabulary, occupying the headline discovery.

column4: Remove the assumption that effective takeover under N:1 remote supervision requires a complete high-frame-rate video stream. If a compressed supervision stream with only keyframes and uncertainty/contact summaries matches takeover success, the full video stream is not load-bearing.
column6: Both crack URLs fail verification, and overlap is medium. The HUD vs full-stream estimand is viable, but offers no clear-accept-level increment over fleet-supervision or compression lines.

column4: Remove the assumption that fleet-scale robot corpora must retain reversible raw RGB or lossless video as the long-term training source of truth. At collection time, use a frozen visual encoder to write feature streams and discard pixels; if feature-only training matches RGB-from-disk under equal episodes and backbones, pixels are not a load-bearing source of truth.
column6: The prior-work review finds medium overlap: ExpReS occupies "frozen fused embeddings replace RGB in the learning loop." The delete-assumption channel has only 1 agreeing crack item and cannot reach SA; it can be repaired into a three-arm falsification of fleet source-of-truth storage.

column4: Remove the assumption that the vision-language tower of a language-conditioned multitask VLA must initialize from internet-VLM weights such as SigLIP/PaliGemma. If a randomly initialized vision tower matches VLM initialization in closed loop under equal data and steps, internet-VLM initialization is not load-bearing.
column6: The prior-work review finds medium overlap: RT-1/R3M occupy pretrained vision vs scratch. Both ACT/DP crack items are partial and are not language-conditioned multitask VLAs. The matched-budget random-vision-tower test is a nearby transfer ablation without a clear-accept increment.

column4: Remove the assumption that real-robot manipulation policies require camera-robot hand-eye extrinsic calibration before deployment. If a wrist camera and relative end-effector actions trained with extrinsic perturbations match a precisely calibrated pipeline with zero deployment calibration, hand-eye calibration is not load-bearing.
column6: CRITICAL F1, high overlap. UMI already states calibration-free deployment using a wrist camera and relative trajectories, and CamVLA occupies policy-side extrinsics-free deployment. The residual A/B comparison is not a clear-accept difference.

column4: Remove the assumption that imitation-learning training sets must filter failed or aborted trajectories and retain only successful demonstrations. With the same number of successful demonstrations, mixing failures directly into BC, not merely as negative examples, should match or beat success-only training, making filtering non-load-bearing.
column6: The prior-work review finds high overlap: AFIL/VINE/RePO occupy the headline that success-only data is harmful and failures belong in VLA training. Delete-assumption break-glass fails because overlap≠low and only 1 crack item agrees. The residual of weighting complete F trajectories equally in the primary BC objective has no clear-accept-level difference from RePO recovery-BC; the minimal falsification is executable, so the idea does not warrant rejection.

column4: More collisions or overshoot after fine-tuning are caused by action-amplitude gain-scale drift that makes the same intended trajectory more aggressive, not catastrophic forgetting of safe demonstrations. A predeployment affine scale-calibration layer should remove most collisions, while full-parameter fine-tuning on an equal amount of safety replay should not be significantly better.
column6: The prior-work review finds medium overlap: affine (g,b) dominance of collisions is occupied by 2606.03724 in a deployment unnorm setting. The combined claim that "more aggressive after SFT indicates gain drift rather than safety forgetting" and the frozen-backbone calibration vs safety-replay discriminator has no direct hit. The repair arm has room for a net increment, but cannot be estimated at clear-accept, so SA is not the default; the falsification is executable.

column4: The closed-loop benefit of a discrete action tokenizer comes from token boundaries forcing explicit policy commitment and suppressing mid-chunk changes of intent, not quantization denoising. A continuous head with a hard commit gate that locks the intent mode within an interval should close the gap, while a continuous head matched to the quantization noise floor should not.
column6: The prior-work review finds medium overlap: ActionCodec occupies the denoising explanation. The hard-commit continuous arm has no same-name search hit, but ACT's open-loop chunks already nearly "lock intent within an interval." The commit-vs-noise-floor discriminator is clean; this is a borderline scientific proposition with a repair arm, and it does not cross the SA increment or standout threshold.

column4: Revisit: build a training-free intrinsic evaluation suite for action tokenizers, including round-trip reconstruction spectra, closed-loop replay fidelity, and perturbation amplification, then test whether intrinsic scores predict downstream VLA rankings. Reread the complete ActionCodec paper, increase the representation-point count and power, and reevaluate unchanged.
column6: The prior-work overlap is medium, and this is pure diagnosis without a repair arm. The headline has no direct hit, but the difference is insufficient for clear-accept, capping the result at AwR.

column4: On tight-tolerance insertion or tightening tasks, failures are caused by the action head lacking "hold duration" as a first-class output and collapsing sustained pressure into a one-frame approach, not large pose error. Under equal tokens, adding a duration head should improve only hold-type subtasks, while expanding the spatial head under an equal budget should not.
column6: The duration-head claim is testable and has no direct hit, but medium neighboring occupancy and misalignment between the headline "pose error is not the main cause" and the experimental arms produce two MAJOR findings.

column4: The load-bearing output of an interactive manipulation world model is not pixel or video decoding but a compact state with relative poses of contactable objects and contact flags. If compact-state WM planning matches a video WM under equal compute, the pixel decoder is not load-bearing.
column6: CRITICAL F1, ImageWAM and isomorphic work occupy "video/pixel decoding is not load-bearing," with high overlap; the residual state definition is insufficient for clear-accept.

column4: Remove the assumption that safe handover in fleet or remote human-in-the-loop operation must first freeze the robot at zero velocity. Under equal human-attention budgets, if a shared-control protocol that keeps the policy running and continuously adds human input as a residual is no worse than stopped takeover in success and collision rates, stopping for handover is not load-bearing.
